But while Jovic converted six out of nine (66.7%), Eala could only manage to convert three (33.3%).\n[...]\nThis is Eala’s second main-draw appearance at Roland Garros, where she also lost in the first round in 2025.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eDays after turning 21, Eala exits French Open singles | Philstar.com\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.philstar.com/sports/2026/05/27/2530752/days-after-turning-21-eala-exits-french-open-singles\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# Days after turning 21, Eala exits French Open singles\n[...]\nMANILA, Philippines — Alex Eala bombed out early in the French Open once more, tasting a 6-4, 6-2 defeat at the hands of her good friend Iva Jovic of the United States in Round 1 yesterday at Court 14 of the Stade Roland Garros in Paris.\n[...]\nEala, WTA No. 37, showed some fight at least in the opener but the world No. 17 Jovic proved just too good to handle with a masterclass in the second set to finish the match in only 100 minutes.\n[...]\nThe 18-year-old Jovic will face her compatriot Emma Navarro, WTA No. 25, in the second round. Navarro dispatched another Southeast Asian bet Janice Tjen (WTA No. 40) of Indonesia, 6-4, 6-3.\n[...]\nBut Jovic proved that it’s still a gritty war on the court in real match, breaking Eala twice in the first set to sprint to a whopping 5-1 start that dictated the tone even with the Filipina’s comeback bid.\n[...]\nEala scored three games in a row, including a breakpoint in the ninth game to strike within 4-5 only for Jovic to shove it back on Eala with another breakpoint on a 40-love dominance for a 1-0 set lead.\n[...]\nIt was all Jovic from there on, breaking two of Eala’s serves once again to cap a 4-0 run from an early gridlock.\n[...]\nOverall, Jovic manufactured 34 winners, including a crisp one in the eighth game of the second set for a 40-15 victory.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eBirthday week magic fades as Alex Eala bows out of French Open against friend Iva Jovic  - Sports News Philippines\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://sportsphnews.com/birthday-week-magic-fades-as-alex-eala-bows-out-of-french-open-against-friend-iva-jovic/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eEala’s 2026 French Open campaign came to an early end on Tuesday night (Manila time) after falling to American teenager and former doubles partner Iva Jovic, 4-6, 2-6, in the opening round at Roland Garros.\n[...]\nThe loss marked another first-round exit for the Filipina star in Paris, but this latest defeat carried a far more personal backdrop.\n[...]\nEala struggled early against the 17th-seeded American, quickly falling behind 1-5 in the opening set as Jovic dictated the pace on the clay surface. Still, the Filipina refused to disappear quietly.\n[...]\nThe world No. 37 mounted a spirited fightback to close the gap to 4-5, briefly threatening to completely change the momentum of the set. But Jovic weathered the push and never fully loosened her grip on the match afterward.\n[...]\nJovic consistently controlled the rallies and kept Eala from finding sustained rhythm throughout the one-hour and 11-minute encounter, eventually sealing a straight-sets victory to move into the second round against fellow American Emma Navarro.\n[...]\nThe Filipina finished her run on red clay with a 4-6 win-loss record across tournaments, highlighted by a breakthrough Round of 32 appearance at the Italian Open earlier this month.\n[...]\n### Friends become first-round foes as Alex Eala opens French Open against Iva Jovic\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlexandra Eala, Victoria Mboko doubles team withdraws at RG\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.thebiglead.com/french-open-2026-alexandra-eala-victoria-mboko-doubles-team-quietly-withdraws/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlexandra Eala, Victoria Mboko doubles team withdraws at RG\n[...]\n# French Open 2026: Alexandra Eala, Victoria Mboko doubles team quietly withdraws\n[...]\nOne of the interesting and highly anticipated women’s doubles pairings at the 2026 French Open was Filipina Alexandra Eala and Canadian Victoria Mboko.\n[...]\nThey were slated to play an intriguing first round match against Leylah Fernandez and Diana Shnaider, both accomplished singles and doubles players but not usually teamed together.\n[...]\nIt appears that the Eala and Mboko team has quietly withdrawn from Roland Garros. Fernandez and Shnaider are now slated to play Oksana Selekhmeteva and Anastasia Zakharova on Wednesday, May 27.\n[...]\nThere has been no official word from the tournament or anyone linked to the players so it is unknown.\n[...]\nEala also suffered a disappointing first round singles loss to Iva Jovic, and perhaps she did not want to stick around in Paris for doubles.\n[...]\nIva Jovic plays a great match to beat Alex Eala 6-4, 6-2 and reach the second round at#RolandGarros. The match took place at the BNP Paribas Arena on red clay [3][4]. This victory marked Rybakina's 29th win of the 2026 season and secured her advancement to the round of 16 [1][2].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eRybakina secures tour-leading 29th victory with win over Eala in Rome\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/news/4501540/elena-rybakina-returns-to-rome-final-16-defeats-alexandra-eala-in-29th-win-of-season\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eRybakina secures tour-leading 29th victory with win over Eala in Rome\n[...]\n# Rybakina secures tour-leading 29th victory with win over Eala in Rome\n[...]\nWorld No. 2 Elena Rybakina booked her spot in the Round of 16 in Rome for the first time since her 2023 title-winning campaign, defeating Alexandra Eala 6-4, 6-3.\n[...]\nWorld No. 2 Elena Rybakina is back into the Rome Round of 16.\n[...]\nAfter an early exit at last year's Internazionali BNL d'Italia, the 2023 champion advanced to the last 16 in Rome for the fourth time in five appearances with a 6-4, 6-3 defeat of Alexandra Eala. Rybakina awaits the winner of Karolina Pliskova and Laura Siegemund in the next round, and boasts a combined 11-1 record against the two.\n[...]\nThough not counted as an unforced error, Rybakina\n[...]\nadvantage of Eala\n[...]\nshe would fault on\n[...]\nfirst. Following an Eala stretch where she won 8-of-9 points to take a 2-1 second set lead, Rybakina won two consecutive games to go up a break once more by powering a forehand on Eala's second serve. She won 45% of Eala's second serve points.\n[...]\nDown 4-3 in the\n[...]\nset, Eala led 30-15 as Rybakina served. She had a prime opportunity on the run at the net to create two break point opportunities. Instead, the ball sailed long, and based on Eala's reaction, she knew that was one she wanted back. Rybakina then held to guarantee herself at least one chance to serve for the match, though she did not need it by breaking Eala in the end.\n[...]\n- Rybakina is now the only player to reach the Round of 16 at all six of the WTA 1000 events this season.\n- With 28 winners Sunday to reach a season total of 925, Rybakina is the first player to eclipse 900 total winners this season.\n- Rybakina has 29 main-draw wins this season, the most of any player, and one more ahead of Mirra Andreeva.\n[...]\nWorld No. 2 Elena Rybakina booked her spot in the Round of 16 in Rome for the first time since her 2023 title-winning campaign, defeating Alexandra Eala 6-4, 6-3.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eItalian Open 2026: Alexandra Eala falls to Elena Rybakina in third round\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.olympics.com/en/news/italian-open-2026-alexandra-eala-falls-to-elena-rybakina-in-third-round-pegula-posts-double-bagel-osaka-rolls-on\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eItalian Open 2026: Alexandra Eala falls to Elena Rybakina in third round\n\n# Italian Open 2026: Alexandra Eala falls to Elena Rybakina in third round; Pegula posts double bagel, Osaka rolls on\n\nWorld No. 2 Rybakina advanced past the 20-year-old Filipino in straight sets in Rome, while Jessica Pegula notched a rare double bagel and Naomi Osaka cruised into the round of 16.\n\nPicture by Al Bello/Getty Images\n\nBy Will Imbo 10 May 2026 16:39 GMT+02 min read\n\nAlexandra Eala's run at the 2026 Internazionali BNL d'Italia ended on Sunday (10 May) as the 20-year-old Filipino fell to World No. 2 Elena Rybakina in the third round in Rome.\n\nRybakina, who beat Aryna Sabalenka to claim her first Australian Open title in January, took the opening set 6-4 at the BNP Paribas Arena and went on to seal the straight-sets victory in their first WTA Tour meeting, 6-4, 6-3. The Kazakh advances to the round of 16, where she'll face the winner of Laura Siegemund vs Karolina Pliskova.\n\nRelive the magic ❄️⛷️ Sign up free for full replays, highlights, and medal moments - anytime.\n\nEala, ranked No. 42, had reached the third round of a WTA 1000 clay-court event for the first time in her career after a 6-4, 6-3 win over Paris 2024 Olympic silver medallist Wang Xinyu in the previous round. Even in defeat, the result marks her best showing in Rome to date, building on her semifinal run in Miami last year and quarterfinal in Dubai earlier this season.\n\nElsewhere in the women's draw, Jessica Pegula registered the first double bagel of her career, needing under an hour to dispatch Rebeka Masarova 6-0, 6-0. [Alex Eala's Birmingham Open win offers proof her game is still growing - ESPN](https://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/49006460/wta-tour-alex-eala-birmingham-win-offers-proof-game-growing)\n2. [Alexandra Eala wins Birmingham Open for 2nd WTA singles title - ESPN](https://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/48992116/alexandra-eala-wins-birmingham-open-2nd-wta-singles-title)\n\nSynthesis:\nAlexandra Eala won the 2026 Lexus Birmingham Open, a WTA 125 grass-court tournament, on June 7, 2026 [1][2][3]. She defeated Nikola Bartunkova in the final with a score of 5-7, 6-3, 7-5 [2][4][3]. This victory marked Eala's second career WTA 125 singles title [4][5].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlex Eala's Birmingham Open win offers proof her game is still growing - ESPN\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/49006460/wta-tour-alex-eala-birmingham-win-offers-proof-game-growing\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlex Eala's Birmingham Open win offers proof her game is still growing - ESPN\n[...]\nAlex Eala celebrates with fans after winning the Birmingham Open at Edgbaston Priory Club. Cameron Smith/Getty Images for LTA\n[...]\nA year ago, Alex Eala walked off the Birmingham Open courts after a first-round exit. On Sunday, she walked off as champion.\n[...]\nEala opened in Birmingham with a dominant win over Priscilla Hon, 6-0, 6-2, and followed up in a tighter battle against Alina Charaeva- winning 6-2, 7-5, having trailed 2-5 in the second set.\n[...]\nShe beat Mananchaya Sawangkaew in the quarterfinals -- 6-3, 6-2 -- controlling most of the match after early exchanges, then came a tough semifinal vs. Rebeka Masarova, whom she defeated 6-2, 4-6, 6-3 after a brief rain delay at 5-2 in the third set.\n[...]\nBy the final, she had already been tested in every way possible, but the match against Nikola Bartunkova featured more challenges amid numerous rain delays.\n[...]\nEala survived break points early, trailed 1-2, then broke to lead 5-3. Bartunkova fought back to 5-5, but Eala broke again in the 11th game before serving out the match to win 5-7, 6-3, 7-5.\n[...]\nThis was Eala's first win over a Czech opponent in 14 meetings. It's not a statistic she'll talk about publicly, but it matters in context. Czech players have long been a tough matchup group for her, and Bartunkova briefly looked like continuing that trend. Instead, Eala closed the loop.\n[...]\nEala's name now sits alongside Billie Jean King, Martina Navratilova, Maria Sharapova, and Petra Kvitova on the list of Birmingham champions, but she didn't put much emphasis on the company.\n[...]\nThe win marks Eala's second WTA 125 title, following her breakthrough in Guadalajara last year, and the victory may see her claim a career-high ranking inside the top 35. But the bigger significance may be timing.\n[...]\nAfter a frustrating clay swing, Birmingham offered something different - proof that her game is still growing.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlexandra Eala wins Birmingham Open for 2nd WTA singles title - ESPN\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/48992116/alexandra-eala-wins-birmingham-open-2nd-wta-singles-title\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlexandra Eala wins Birmingham Open for 2nd WTA singles title - ESPN\n\nSkip to main content Skip to navigation\n\nBIRMINGHAM, England -- Alexandra Eala claimed her second WTA 125 singles title with a hard-fought victory in the Birmingham Open final against doubles partner Nikola Bartunkova on Sunday.\n\nThe top-seeded Eala battled back from a set down to beat Bartunkova 5-7, 6-3, 7-5.\n\nEala, who turned 21 last month, has become a worldwide sensation among the large Filipino diaspora ever since her breakthrough run to the Miami Open semifinals last year.\n\nHer fan base then expanded again when she beat Clara Tauson in a long third-set tiebreaker in the first round at the U.S. Open for her first win in a major.\n\nTwo weeks later, Eala was the first woman representing the Philippines to win a Challenger-level WTA title when she triumphed at the Guadalajara 125 Open.\n\nEala, who is based at the Rafa Nadal Academy in Spain, attracted more attention at this year's Australian Open - the closest Grand Slam to the Philippines.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

Muchová won the match in straight sets, 6-0, 6-2, in exactly one hour to advance to the quarterfinals [2][3].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eMiami Open 2026: Karolína Muchová beats Alex Eala to reach quarter-finals\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.olympics.com/en/news/miami-open-2026-karolina-muchova-beats-alex-eala-to-reach-quarter-finals\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eMiami Open 2026: Karolína Muchová beats Alex Eala to reach quarter-finals\n\n# Miami Open 2026: Karolína Muchová beats Alex Eala to reach quarter-finals\n\nThe Czech produced a dominant Round of 16 display on 23 March, converting five of five break points to end Eala’s impressive Miami Open run.\n\nPicture by Matthew Stockman/Getty Images\n\nBy Will Imbo\n\nKarolína Muchová produced one of the most emphatic performances of the day at the Miami Open, overpowering rising Filipina tennis star Alex Eala 6-0, 6-2 in the Round of 16 on Grandstand.\n\nThe result sent the Czech, ranked No. 14 by the WTA, into the final eight and brought an end to another notable Miami campaign for Eala, whose rise over the past year has made her one of the most closely watched young players on the tour.\n\nMuchová, a 2023 Roland Garros finalist and two-time US Open semi-finalist, moved through the opening set without dropping a game and closed the match in straight sets, winning 52 points to 26 and converting all five break points she earned.\n\nEala, who arrived in Miami at a career-high No. 29 in the WTA rankings, got on the board in the second set but was unable to change the direction of the match. The 20-year-old, who is the first player from her country to reach a WTA 1000 semi-final and break into the top 100, exits in the Round of 16, while Muchová moves on to the quarter-finals.\n\nRelive the magic ❄️⛷️ Sign up free for full replays, highlights, and medal moments - anytime.\n\n15 - 29 MarchTennis | Miami Open | United States of AmericaTennis\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eKarolina Muchova Dominates Alexandra Eala at 2026 Miami Open\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://justwomenssports.com/reads/karolina-muchova-alexandra-eala-miami-open-quarterfinals-2026-results/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eKarolina Muchova Dominates Alexandra Eala at 2026 Miami Open\n\nTop Events\n\nTop Events NWSL WNBA NCAAW LPGA WTA\n\n# Karolina Muchova Sends Alexandra Eala Packing in Dominant Miami Open Win\n\nAlexandra Eala fell to Karolina Muchova in straight sets at the 2026 Miami Open's Round of 16. (Al Bello/Getty Images)\n\nKarolina Muchova delivered a dominant performance to defeat Alexandra Eala 6-0, 6-2 in exactly one hour on Monday, reaching her first-ever Miami Open quarterfinal.\n\nThe Doha champion crushed last year's semifinalist with clinical precision, firing 20 winners against just 11 unforced errors while the Filipina tennis star managed only seven winners and 13 unforced errors.\n\n\"It felt pretty good,\" Muchova said postmatch. \"I mean, you get nervous here and there when it's going — I don't want to say easy, but when the score is in your favor you have to be cautious.\"\n\nThe opening set lasted just 22 minutes. Muchova conceded only six points in the bagel set — two on serve and four on return, with Eala failing to reach game point until the third game of the second set.\n\nThe world No. 6 raced through the first 10 games without reply to lead 6-0, 4-0. A possibility of her first tour-level double bagel loomed before a wild drive volley enabled Eala to finally hold serve.\n\nMuchova never faced a break point in the match, dropping only eight points total on her serve — two in the first set and six in the second.\n\nThe Czech international improved her 2026 season record to 16-3. Only five players have won more tour-level matches this year: Elina Svitolina, Elena Rybakina, Aryna Sabalenka, Jessica Pegula and Victoria Mboko.\n\nShe will face either No. 8 seed Mirra Andreeva or No. 10 seed Mboko in the quarterfinals. A matchup with Mboko would be a rematch of the Doha final, which Muchova won 6-4, 7-5.\n\nDespite the loss, Alexandra Eala moved three spots up the WTA singles ranking after making it the Miami Open's Round of 16.\n\nCookies\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eMuchova dismisses Eala in exactly one hour to reach Miami quarters\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/news/4475068/muchova-dismisses-eala-in-exactly-one-hour-to-reach-miami-quarters\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eMuchova dismisses Eala in exactly one hour to reach Miami quarters\n[...]\n# Muchova dismisses Eala in exactly one hour to reach Miami quarters\n[...]\nKarolina Muchova dropped just two games against Alexandra Eala to reach her first Miami Open quarterfinal.\n[...]\nDoha champion Karolina Muchova kicked off the Miami Open Round of 16 with a masterclass, defeating last year's semifinalist Alexandra Eala 6-0, 6-2 in exactly an hour to reach her third quarterfinal of 2026.\n[...]\nIn the first Miami quarterfinal of her career, Muchova will next face No. 10 seed Victoria Mboko in a rematch of last month's Doha final, which the Czech won 6-4, 7-5. the Rome Round of 16.\n[...]\nRome: Scores| Draws| Order of play\n[...]\nAfter an early exit at last year's Internazionali BNL d'Italia, the 2023 champion advanced to the last 16 in Rome for the fourth time in five appearances with a 6-4, 6-3 defeat of Alexandra Eala. Rybakina awaits the winner of Karolina Pliskova and Laura Siegemund in the next round, and boasts a combined 11-1 record against the two.\n[...]\nRybakina took advantage\n[...]\nala stretch where she won\n[...]\n8-of-9 points to take a 2-1 second set lead, Rybakina won two consecutive games to go up\n[...]\nbreak once more by powering a forehand on Eala\n[...]\ns second serve\n[...]\nShe won 45% of Eala's second serve points\n[...]\nDown 4-3 in the second set, Eala led 30-15 as Rybakina served. Rybakina then held to guarantee herself at least one chance to serve for the match, though she did not need it by breaking Eala in the end.\n[...]\n- Rybakina is now the only player to reach the Round of 16 at all six of the WTA 1000 events this season.\n- With 28 winners Sunday to reach a season total of 925, Rybakina is the first player to eclipse 900 total winners this season.\n- Rybakina has 29 main-draw wins this season, the most of any player, and one more ahead of Mirra Andreeva.\n[...]\nWorld No. 2 Elena Rybakina booked her spot in the Round of 16 in Rome for the first time since her 2023 title-winning campaign, defeating Alexandra Eala 6-4, 6-3.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eItalian Open 2026: Alexandra Eala falls to Elena Rybakina in third round\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.olympics.com/en/news/italian-open-2026-alexandra-eala-falls-to-elena-rybakina-in-third-round-pegula-posts-double-bagel-osaka-rolls-on\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eItalian Open 2026: Alexandra Eala falls to Elena Rybakina in third round\n\n# Italian Open 2026: Alexandra Eala falls to Elena Rybakina in third round; Pegula posts double bagel, Osaka rolls on\n\nWorld No. 2 Rybakina advanced past the 20-year-old Filipino in straight sets in Rome, while Jessica Pegula notched a rare double bagel and Naomi Osaka cruised into the round of 16.\n\nPicture by Al Bello/Getty Images\n\nBy Will Imbo 10 May 2026 16:39 GMT+02 min read\n\nAlexandra Eala's run at the 2026 Internazionali BNL d'Italia ended on Sunday (10 May) as the 20-year-old Filipino fell to World No. 2 Elena Rybakina in the third round in Rome.\n\nRybakina, who beat Aryna Sabalenka to claim her first Australian Open title in January, took the opening set 6-4 at the BNP Paribas Arena and went on to seal the straight-sets victory in their first WTA Tour meeting, 6-4, 6-3. The Kazakh advances to the round of 16, where she'll face the winner of Laura Siegemund vs Karolina Pliskova.\n\nRelive the magic ❄️⛷️ Sign up free for full replays, highlights, and medal moments - anytime.\n\nEala, ranked No. 42, had reached the third round of a WTA 1000 clay-court event for the first time in her career after a 6-4, 6-3 win over Paris 2024 Olympic silver medallist Wang Xinyu in the previous round. Even in defeat, the result marks her best showing in Rome to date, building on her semifinal run in Miami last year and quarterfinal in Dubai earlier this season.\n\nElsewhere in the women's draw, Jessica Pegula registered the first double bagel of her career, needing under an hour to dispatch Rebeka Masarova 6-0, 6-0. Naomi Osaka also booked her place in the round of 16 for a third straight year in Rome, dispatching No. 19 seed Diana Shnaider 6-1, 6-2.\n\n28 April - 17 MayTennis | Internazionali BNL d’Italia | Rome | ItalyTennis\n\n## New original series\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eElena Rybakina tops Alexandra Eala 6-4, 6-3 at WTA 1000 Rome\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.sofascore.com/news/elena-rybakina-tops-alexandra-eala-6-4-6-3\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eElena Rybakina tops Alexandra Eala 6-4, 6-3 at WTA 1000 Rome\n[...]\nWorld No. 2 Elena Rybakina moved into the Round of 16 at the WTA 1000 Rome with a straight-sets win over Alexandra Eala, 6-4, 6-3. [‘SEAsters’ Eala and Tjen exit Abu Dhabi Open doubles | The Manila Times](https://www.manilatimes.net/2026/02/06/sports/seasters-eala-and-tjen-exit-abu-dhabi-open-doubles/2273423)\n5. [Eala, Tjen bow out of Abu Dhabi Open doubles - Manila Standard](https://manilastandard.net/sports/314700769/eala-tjen-bow-out-of-abu-dhabi-open-doubles.html)\n\nSynthesis:\nAlex Eala and Janice Tjen were eliminated in the doubles semifinals of the 2026 Mubadala Abu Dhabi Open on Friday, February 6, 2026 [1][2][3]. They lost to Tereza Mihalikova and Olivia Nicholls in straight sets, 4-6, 2-6 [3][4][5]. Despite the loss, their performance marked a breakthrough as the first Southeast Asian duo to reach the semifinals of a WTA 500 tournament in their first event playing together [6].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlex Eala, Janice Tjen bow out of Abu Dhabi Open doubles | GMA News Online\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.gmanetwork.com/news/sports/othersports/975687/alex-eala-janice-tjen-bow-out-of-abu-dhabi-open-doubles/story/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlex Eala, Janice Tjen bow out of Abu Dhabi Open doubles | GMA News Online\n\nWe use cookies to ensure you get the best browsing experience. By continued use, you agree to our privacy policy and accept our use of such cookies. For further information, click FIND OUT MORE.\n\nI AGREE FIND OUT MORE\n\nADVERTISEMENT\n\nFiltered By: Sports\n\nSports\n\nPublished February 6, 2026 9:51pm\n\n+ Add GMA on Google\n\nPhoto: Abu Dhabi Open/IG\n\nAlexandra “Alex” Eala of the Philippines and Janice Tjen of Indonesia bowed out of the Abu Dhabi Open doubles tournament after falling to the tandem of Tereza Mihalikova (Slovakia) and Olivia Nicholls (Great Britain), 6-4, 6-2, in the semifinals on Friday.\n\nView this post on Instagram\n\nA post shared by Mubadala Abu Dhabi Open (@mubadalaabudhabiopen)\n\nThe match lasted one hour and 16 minutes, as the Southeast Asian pair capped their run in the event.\n\nEala and Tjen stayed close in the opening set and were within striking distance at 5-4, before Mihalikova and Nicholls took the first set.\n\nIn the second set, Eala and Tjen fell behind 0-3 before finding a breakthrough. However, the doubles powerhouse asserted its dominance to secure a spot in the finals.\n\nEala’s Abu Dhabi Open campaign also came to an end in singles play on Thursday after she fell to Ekaterina Alexandrova in the quarterfinals.\n\nThe Filipina ace is expected to head to Doha next for the Qatar Open, which begins on Sunday.\n\nEala has been competing since early January, reaching the semifinals of the ASB Classic. She was later crowned champion of the Kooyong Classic exhibition tournament before bowing out in the first round of the Australian Open to Alycia Parks.\n\nMost recently, Eala competed on home soil, where she fell to Camila Osorio in the quarterfinals.\n\n—JKC, GMA Integraed News\n\nTags: Alex Eala, Janice Tjen, Abu Dhabi Open\n\nMore Videos\n\nMost Popular\n\nLOADING CONTENT\n\nRETRY LOADING\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eTennis: Alex Eala, Janice Tjen exit Abu Dhabi Open doubles tournament | ABS-CBN Sports\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.abs-cbn.com/sports/othersports/2026/2/6/tennis-alex-eala-janice-tjen-exit-abu-dhabi-open-doubles-tournament-2135\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eTennis: Alex Eala, Janice Tjen exit Abu Dhabi Open doubles tournament | ABS-CBN Sports\n\n## Tennis: Alex Eala, Janice Tjen exit Abu Dhabi Open doubles tournament\n\nADVERTISEMENT\n\nHEADLINES:\n\n|\n\n#### News\n\n#### Entertainment\n\n#### Lifestyle\n\n#### Sports\n\n#### Metro.Style\n\n#### More\n\n#### Sudoku\n\n#### Word of the Day\n\n#### Lotto\n\n#### Horoscope\n\nHEADLINES:\n\n|\n\n#### Sports\n\n# Tennis: Alex Eala, Janice Tjen exit Abu Dhabi Open doubles tournament\n\n#### Kennedy Caacbay,\n\n#### Dyan Castillejo,\n\n#### ABS-CBN News\n\n#### Published February 06, 2026 09:35 PM PHT\n\n#### |\n\n#### Updated February 06, 2026 11:06 PM PHT\n\n---\n\nADVERTISEMENT\n\nADVERTISEMENT\n\n#### Read More:\n\n#### abssports\n\n|\n\n#### tennis\n\n|\n\n#### Alex Eala\n\n|\n\n#### Abu Dhabi Open\n\n|\n\n#### Janice Tjen\n\n|\n\n#### ABSnews\n\n---\n\n#### ABS-CBN is the leading media and entertainment company in the Philippines, offering quality content across TV, radio, digital, and film. Dhabi Open semis | Philstar.com\n[...]\n# Eala, Tjen get boot in Abu Dhabi Open semis\n[...]\nFebruary 6, 2026 | 9:47pm\n[...]\nAlex Eala and Janice Tjen\n[...]\nMANILA, Philippines -- The partnership of rising Southeast Asian tennisters Alex Eala and Janice Tjen has ended in the Mubadala Abu Dhabi Open.\n[...]\nEala and Tjen bowed out of the WTA 500 tournament’s doubles’ semifinals after getting bounced by Tereza Mihalikova and Olivia Nicholls, 4-6, 2-6, Friday evening (Manila time).\n[...]\nThe Filipino and Indonesian tennis stars absorbed the loss in an hour and 16 minutes, as the fourth-seeded pair of Mihalikova and Nicholls was formidable.\n[...]\nThe first set was close, with Eala and Tjen tying things up at 4-all.\n[...]\nBut Mihalikova and Nicholls held their serve, then broke their opponent’s, to grab the first set, 6-4.\n[...]\nThey carried the momentum to the second service, going up 3-0, which proved to be insurmountable for the younger pair.\n[...]\nWhile the Southeast Asian players punched in two aces, they were only able to win 18 receiving points compared to their opponent’s 21.\n[...]\nThey also won 30 service points against the Slovakian-British duo’s 36.\n[...]\nBefore being shown the door, the Eala-Tjen duo defeated Leylah Fernandez and Kristina Mladenovic in the first round, followed by a huge upset win over Zhang Shuai and Cristina Bucsa.\n[...]\nEala, ranked World No. 45 in singles’ action, also exited the same WTA 500 tourney’s one-on-one edition after getting dominated by World No. 11 Ekaterina Alexandrova in the quarterfinals.\n[...]\nThe Filipina will now set her sights to the Qatar Totalenergies Open, which will kick off on February 8.\n[...]\n#### Eala, Tjen enter semis in Abu Dhabi Open doubles\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003e‘SEAsters’ Eala and Tjen exit Abu Dhabi Open doubles | The Manila Tjen exit Abu Dhabi Open doubles | The Manila Times\n\nThe Manila Times 500\n\n# ‘SEAsters’ Eala and Tjen exit Abu Dhabi Open doubles\n\nMANILA, Philippines — Filipino tennis sensation Alex Eala’s campaign in the 2026 Abu Dhabi Open has come to an end after a semifinal exit in the doubles semifinals with Janice Tjen of Indonesia on Friday night (Manila time) at Zayed Sports Center.\n\nThe two Southeast Asian (SEA) rising stars were overpowered by Tereza Mihalikova of Slovenia and Olivia Nicholls of United Kingdom, 6-4, 6-2.\n\nPremium + Digital Edition\n\nAd-free access\n\nP 80 per month Read More.\n\nClose\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEala, Tjen bow out of Abu Dhabi Open doubles - Manila Standard\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://manilastandard.net/sports/314700769/eala-tjen-bow-out-of-abu-dhabi-open-doubles.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eEala, Tjen bow out of Abu Dhabi Open doubles - Manila Standard\n[...]\nupdated February 7,\n[...]\n026\n[...]\n# Eala, Tjen bow out of Abu Dhabi Open doubles\n[...]\nEnd of the road for Alex Eala of the Philippines and Janice Tjen of Indonesia after their doubles semifinal loss at the WTA 500 Mubadala Abu Dhabi Open. (Courtesy of Mon Catajan's screengrab)\n[...]\nFilipina Alex Eala and Indonesia’s Janice Tjen bowed out of the doubles competition of the WTA 500 Mubadala Abu Dhabi Open after a 4-6, 2-6 loss to Slovak Tereza Mihalikova and British partner Olivia Nicholls on Friday night (Manila time).\n[...]\nEala and Tjen, affectionately dubbed the “SEAsters” by fans, put up a spirited fight against the experienced European pair, keeping the opening set close before a large crowd at the Zayed Sports City International Tennis Centre’s Stadium Court.\n[...]\nThe Southeast Asian duo had a chance to break serve at 4-all in the first set, but a wide crosscourt winner from Nicholls in the ninth game and a pair of late errors by Tjen proved costly, allowing Mihalikova and Nicholls to take the set.\n[...]\nMomentum stayed with the Slovak-British tandem in the second set as they raced to a 3-0 lead and controlled the remainder of the 76-minute match. Eala and Tjen mounted a brief comeback to trail 2-5, but Nicholls sealed the victory with a decisive smash at the net.\n[...]\nDespite the loss, Eala and Tjen shared $21,720 in prize money and earned 195 WTA points for their run in the tournament.\n[...]\nEala will next compete in the WTA 1000 Qatar Open, where the world No. 45 has secured a spot in the main draw. The tournament runs from February 8 to 14.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",
      "title": "Web search results"
    }
  ],
  "rejected": false
}

{

      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [Alexandra Eala wins Birmingham Open for 2nd WTA singles title - ESPN](https://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/48992116/alexandra-eala-wins-birmingham-open-2nd-wta-singles-title)\n2. [Eala vs. Bartunkova | Final Lexus Birmingham Open 2026 | WTA Official](https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/1126/birmingham-125/2026/scores/LS001)\n\nSynthesis:\nIn the 2026 Lexus Birmingham Open final, Alexandra Eala defeated Nikola Bartunkova with a score of 5-7, 6-3, 7-5 [1][2][3].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlexandra Eala wins Birmingham Open for 2nd WTA singles title - ESPN\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/48992116/alexandra-eala-wins-birmingham-open-2nd-wta-singles-title\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlexandra Eala wins Birmingham Open for 2nd WTA singles title - ESPN\n\nSkip to main content Skip to navigation\n\nBIRMINGHAM, England -- Alexandra Eala claimed her second WTA 125 singles title with a hard-fought victory in the Birmingham Open final against doubles partner Nikola Bartunkova on Sunday.\n\nThe top-seeded Eala battled back from a set down to beat Bartunkova 5-7, 6-3, 7-5.\n\nEala, who turned 21 last month, has become a worldwide sensation among the large Filipino diaspora ever since her breakthrough run to the Miami Open semifinals last year.\n\nHer fan base then expanded again when she beat Clara Tauson in a long third-set tiebreaker in the first round at the U.S. Open for her first win in a major.\n\nTwo weeks later, Eala was the first woman representing the Philippines to win a Challenger-level WTA title when she triumphed at the Guadalajara 125 Open.\n\nEala, who is based at the Rafa Nadal Academy in Spain, attracted more attention at this year's Australian Open - the closest Grand Slam to the Philippines.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEala vs. Bartunkova | Final Lexus Birmingham Open 2026 | WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/1126/birmingham-125/2026/scores/LS001\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eEala vs. Bartunkova | Final Lexus Birmingham Open 2026 | WTA Official\n\nGo back to the home page\n\n-\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",
      "title": "Web search results"

**Alex Eala Pro Career Tracker** is updated through the 2026 French Open and Birmingham grass season.

### Rankings
- **Doubles career high:** No. 99 → **No. 88** (May 4, 2026, WTA)
- **Singles career high:** unchanged at **No. 29**

### Tournament results added
- **Birmingham (WTA 125)** — Winner (5-7, 6-3, 7-5 vs. Bartunkova); second career WTA title, first on grass
- **Abu Dhabi doubles (WTA 500)** — Semifinals with Janice Tjen

### Grand Slam
- **French Open 2026** — 1st Round loss to Iva Jovic (4-6, 2-6)

### Head-to-head (promoted from watch list)
- **Coco Gauff** — Indian Wells Win (Retired), Dubai Loss
- **Elena Rybakina** — Italian Open Loss (with WTA highlights embed)

### Head-to-head (updated)
- **Ekaterina Alexandrova** — Abu Dhabi 2026 Loss
- **Linda Noskova** — Indian Wells 2026 Loss
- **Karolina Muchova** — Miami 2026 Loss

### Watch list
- Removed **Coco Gauff** and **Elena Rybakina** (now played)

### Not added (by design)
- **Indian Wells / Miami R16** — below the QF+ threshold for tournament results (still logged in H2H)
- **Queen’s Club, Nottingham** — did not reach QF
- **Wimbledon 2026** — not yet played
- **French Open 2026 doubles** — withdrew before first round

No commit was made. Say if you want this committed or if Wimbledon results should be added once the draw starts.

Eala becomes the first woman from the Philippines to break into the Top 40 | Tennis.com\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala continues her historic rise this week, becoming the first woman from the Philippines to break into the Top 40 in WTA rankings history, which dates all the way back to 1975.\n[...]\nShe rises from No. 45 to No. 40 today following her run to the quarterfinals of Abu Dhabi, which was actually her first time reaching the quarterfinals of a WTA 500-level event.\n[...]\nIt was her fifth time reaching the quarterfinals at a WTA event at any level and they’ve all come in the last 11 months—her first three last year with her run to the semis of Miami (WTA 1000), the final of Eastbourne (WTA 250) and the quarters in Sao Paulo (WTA 250), and two this year with a semifinal in Auckland (WTA 250) and now a quarterfinal in Abu Dhabi (WTA 500).\n[...]\nThe 20-year-old was already the first woman from the Philippines to break into both the Top 100 and the Top 50 when she cracked those ranking classes last year, in March and November, respectively. Regarding the 2025 Guadalajara tournament, Alexandra Eala won the WTA 125 event held earlier in September 2025 [3]. She is not the winner of the WTA 500 Guadalajara Open held later that month, which was won by Iva Jovic [4].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlexandra Eala thanks fans after Miami Open run ends in semifinal | Tennis.com\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennis.com/baseline/articles/alexandra-eala-thanks-fans-miami-open-semifinal-social-media\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlexandra Eala thanks fans after Miami Open run ends in semifinal | Tennis.com\n[...]\n## Alexandra Eala thanks fans after Miami Open run ends in semifinal\n[...]\n# Alexandra Eala thanks fans after Miami Open run ends in semifinal\n[...]\n##### MATCH POINT: Jessica Pegula perseveres past teen Alexandra Eala to reach first Miami Open final\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala checked in with fans Friday following her Miami Open exit.\n[...]\n“A heartfelt thank you to everybody on here for all the love and sweet messages that you guys have sent over the past few days,” the 19-year-old wrote on her Instagram Stories. “It will take time for me to get back to you all, but! want you guys to know I appreciate every single one of you.”\n[...]\nRead more: Jessica Pegula ends Alexandra Eala's fairytale run in Miami\n[...]\nEala enjoyed a Cinderella story at the Hard Rock Stadium, becoming the first player from the Philippines to defeat not one but two Top 10 players, scoring wins over Madison Keys and Iga Swiatek en route to her first WTA 1000 semifinal.\n[...]\nThe Rafael Nadal Academy graduate came within one set of the final when she pushed Jessica Pegula in three sets, but ultimately fell, 7-6 (3), 5-7, 6-3, nonetheless earning a warm ovation from the Miami crowd.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlexandra Eala's breakthrough run to the Miami Open semifinals: 10 of the best stats and records | Tennis.com\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennis.com/news/articles/filipina-teenager-alexandra-eala-miami-breakthrough-best-stats-records\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlexandra Eala's breakthrough run to the Miami Open semifinals: 10 of the best stats and records | Tennis.com\n[...]\nWildcard Alexandra Eala Upsets Iga Swiatek in the Miami Quarterfinals | TC Live5:49\n[...]\nFilipina teenager Alexandra Eala was the breakthrough star of the Miami Open this year, coming from virtually out of nowhere to make it all the way to the semifinals of the WTA 1000 tournament, taking out top player after top player—and rewriting all kinds of history—along the way.\n[...]\nShe became the first woman from the Philippines to defeat a Grand Slam champion at a tour-level event in the Open Era. And she did it three times: 2017 Roland Garros champion Jelena Ostapenko in the second round, 2025 Australian Open champion Madison Keys in the third round and, most famously, five-time Grand Slam winner Iga Swiatek in the quarterfinals.\n[...]\nThose were actually her first three career matches against Top 25 players. And she wasn’t far from improving to 4-0 against Top 25 players, falling to No. 4-ranked Jessica Pegula in the semifinals, 7-6 (3), 5-7, 6-3.\n[...]\nAnd just how out of nowhere was her run? Eala was 2-14 in her career in tour-level matches going into Miami. She had never even won back-to-back tour-level matches in her career before, but won four in a row—over Katie Volynets, Ostapenko, Keys and Swiatek—en route to the semifinals.\n[...]\nwho was ranked\n[...]\nby defeating Swiatek,\n[...]\nbecame the lowest-ranked\n[...]\n’s the\n[...]\nbeat No.\n[...]\n2-ranked\n[...]\nova there in\n[...]\nShe equaled the best result\n[...]\n. Justine Henin also made\n[...]\nas did Azarenka in\n[...]\nShe also became first wild card to defeat three Grand Slam champions in straight sets at a single WTA event. She rallied from 4-0 down in the first set for a 7-6 (2), 7-5 win over Ostapenko, then beat Keys, 6-4, 6-2, then rallied from 4-2 down in the second set for a 6-2, 7-5 win over Swiatek.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eIndonesian trailblazer Janice Tjen stuns Alexandra Eala in Sao Paulo QF | Tennis.com\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennis.com/news/articles/indonesian-trailblazer-janice-tjen-stuns-alexandra-eala-in-sao-paulo-qf\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eJanice Tjen continued a spectacular breakthrough onto the Hologic WTA Tour at the SP Open in Sao Paulo, stunning Alexandra Eala, 6-4, 6-1 to reach the semifinals.\n[...]\nWaiting for her there was an in-form Eala, fresh off her first WTA 125K title run at the Guadalajara 125 Open earlier this month. The 20-year-old from the Philippines has been one of the biggest stories of the 2025 season, reaching the Miami Open semifinals with wins over Jelena Ostapenko, Madison Keys, and Iga Swiatek. She made her first WTA final three months later on grass in Eastbourne and dropped just two sets en route to the title in Guadalajara, rallying from a set down to defeat Pann Udvardy in the final.\n[...]\n6 6\n[...]\n4 1\n[...]\nShe made quick work of her SP Open opposition to reach the quarterfinals and took a quick 2-0 lead on Tjen when their match began on Friday. But Tjen quickly caught fire from there, winning 12 of the final 15 games to surge into the semifinal in just over an hour.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eTeen queens crowned: Iva Jovic, Tiantsoa Rakotomanga Rajaonah claim first WTA titles | Tennis.com\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennis.com/news/articles/teen-queens-crowned-iva-jovic-tiantsoa-rakotomanga-rajaonah-claim-first-wta-titles\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# Teen queens crowned: Iva Jovic, Tiantsoa Rakotomanga Rajaonah claim first WTA titles\n[...]\nAmerican Jovic, 17, triumphed in Guadalajara while French 19-year-old Rakotomanga Rajaonah lifted the trophy in Brazil.\n[...]\nTwo teeange dreams were realized this week on the WTA tour. American Iva Jovic, 17, and French 19-year-old Tiantsoa Rakotomanga Rajaonah won the first tour-level singles titles of their respective careers at the WTA 500 in Guadalajara and the WTA 250 in Sao Paulo.\n[...]\nIn two finals that assured first-time champions no matter the result, Jovic topped Colombia's Emiliana Arango 6-4, 6-1, while Rakotomanga Rajaonah defeated Indonesia's Janice Tjen 6-4, 6-3.\n[...]\nThe two youngsters, both of whom were unseeded at their events, took advantage of wide-open draws the week after the US Open. The Guadalajara Open Akron lost No. 1 and No. 2 seeds Elise Mertens and Veronika Kudermetova in the second round, while the SP Open's top seed was world No. 27 Beatriz Haddad Maia. Though six of the eight seeds reached the quarterfinals in Sao Paulo, only two reached the semis: No. 5 Renata Zarazua, beaten by Rakotomanga Rajaonah, and No. 8 Francesca Jones, beaten by Tjen.\n[...]\nAdditionally, both victorious players saved match points in previous rounds before even reaching the final.\n[...]\nIn her first-round match against Mexico's Ana Sofia Sanchez, Rakotomanga Rajaonah rallied from a 5-0 deficit in the final set, and saved three match points. Jovic, meanwhile, denied Victoria Jimenez Kasintseva of Andorra a match point in a 6-3, 3-6, 7-6(6) quarterfinal.\n[...]\nThe youngest American to win a tour singles title since Coco Gauff triumphed on clay in Parma four years ago, Jovic's win also makes her the youngest winner of any WTA singles title this year at 17 years, 283 days old; she supplants Mirra Andreeva, who won the Dubai Duty Free Tennis Championships in February at 17 years, 299 days old.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eMATCH POINT: Jessica Pegula perseveres past teen Alexandra Eala to reach first Miami Open final\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennis.com/news/videos/match-point-jessica-pegula-perseveres-past-breakthrough-teen-alexandra-eala-to-reach-first-miami-open-final\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eMATCH POINT: Jessica Pegula perseveres past teen Alexandra Eala to reach first Miami Open final | Tennis.com\n[![Tennis logo](https://www.tennis.com/assets/logos/tennis-logo-desktop.svg)](https://www.tennis.com/)\n## Advertising\nMiami, USA\nMar 28, 2025\n# MATCH POINT: Jessica Pegula perseveres past teen Alexandra Eala to reach first Miami Open final\nMar 28, 2025\n![](https://images.tennis.com/image/private/t_vs-fe-thumb/tenniscom-prd/hiv5pkgitn1feqg5qqdc)\nMiami, USA###### INTERVIEW: Jakub Mensik will set new goals after winning the Miami Open\nMar 31, 2025\n![](https://images.tennis.com/image/private/t_vs-fe-thumb/tenniscom-prd/e51fyu9ybhs8fsjjq454)\nMiami, USA###### SPEECH: Novak Djokovic lauds Jakub Mensik after Miami Open final loss\nMar 31, 2025\n![](https://images.tennis.com/image/private/t_vs-fe-thumb/tenniscom-prd/hh8y4rngwcda2pe4ncxo)\nMiami, USA###### CHAMPIONSHIP POINT: Jakub Mensik denies Novak Djokovic 100th singles title with Miami triumph\nMar 31, 2025\n![](https://images.tennis.com/image/private/t_vs-fe-thumb/tenniscom-prd/xjpeljkw8ee5qoksc3y2)\nMiami, USA###### Cheers! Watch Jessica Pegula steal Aryna Sabalenka's signature cocktail after Miami final\nMar 29, 2025\n![](https://images.tennis.com/image/private/t_vs-fe-thumb/tenniscom-prd/ffzdh87tmvymdv2ztntb)\nMiami, USA###### Aryna Sabalenka would share the Miami Open trophy with Jessica Pegula if she could\nMar 29, 2025\nSee More Results\nTennis Channel Plus LogoTennis Channel LogoWinnerBallInfoSPINNERGRAND SLAM LOGOFULLSCREEN WHITEFULLSCREENLATEST DOCUMENTSDOWNLOADDOCUMENTALBUMLIVE BLOGVISUAL STORIES\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

Eastbourne Open 2025: Maya Joint defeats Alexandra Eala to claim first title on grass | LTA\n[...]\n# Lexus Eastbourne Open 2025: Maya Joint defeats Alexandra Eala to claim first title on grass\n[...]\n28 June 2025 • 3 minute read\n[...]\nOur 2025 Lexus Eastbourne Open women’s champion has been crowned as Maya Joint ousted Alexandra Eala to lift her second WTA tour-level title and first on grass.\n[...]\nToday's match marked the youngest Eastbourne final since 1981 where an 18-year-old Tracy Austin beat 16-year-old Andrea Jaeger.\n[...]\n- First round: won vs Ons Jabeur (TUN) 7-5, 6-2\n- Second round: won vs Emma Raducanu (GBR) (7) 4-6, 6-1, 7-6(4)\n- Quarter-final: won vs Anna Blinkova 6-4, 7-5\n- Semi-final: won vs Anastasia Pavlyuchenkova 7-5, 6-3\n- Final: won vs Alexandra Eala (PHI) 6-4, 1-6 7-6(10)\n[...]\nIt's\n[...]\n\"This is\n[...]\nfirst WTA final - it's a big deal for\n[...]\nand for my country too because it’s historic, I guess that’s also why\n[...]\n’m so emotional. Wimbledon is next week so hopefully\n[...]\n’ll forget about this match soon.”\n[...]\nHeading into the career-first meeting between the two, it’s safe to say Joint had the advantage having previously competed in, and won, a WTA final back in May in Rabat.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eJoint saves four championship points to beat Eala for Eastbourne title\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/news/4298129/joint-saves-four-championship-points-defeats-eala-to-win-eastbourne-title\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eto beat Eala for Eastbourne title\n[...]\n3m read\n[...]\n28 Jun 2025 11mo ago\n[...]\nIn a championship showdown between two of the Hologic WTA Tour's brightest young talents, Maya Joint fended off four championship points and outlasted Alexandra Eala to capture the Lexus Eastbourne Open title. It is Joint's second WTA singles title, having already prevailed in Rabat on clay last month.\n[...]\nMaya Joint rallied to win one of the year’s toughest matches at the Lexus Eastbourne Open on Saturday, securing her second Hologic WTA Tour singles title of 2025.\n[...]\nIn a matchup of two of the Hologic WTA Tour’s brightest young talents, 19-year-old Maya Joint of Australia saved four championship points to edge 20-year-old qualifier Alexandra Eala of the Philippines 6-4, 1-6, 7-6 (10) in Saturday’s final.\n[...]\nTwo titles in two months: Joint has now finished as the singles champion in two of her last four WTA events -- and on two distinctly different surfaces. She took the title on the red clay of Rabat in May, and has now backed it up with the grass-court title on the storied lawns of Eastbourne.\n[...]\nThree more Eala championship points came and went as Joint found well-timed combos of outrageous speed and power plays to keep her in the match. After forcing an error with a forehand, Joint had saved four match points and it was 10-10.\n[...]\nEala then misfired wide with a backhand to give Joint her second championship point at 11-10. There, the contest concluded, with one last Joint backhand winner sealing an emotional match for both youngsters.\n[...]\nIn a championship showdown between two of the Hologic WTA Tour's brightest young talents, Maya Joint fended off four championship points and outlasted Alexandra Eala to capture the Lexus Eastbourne Open title. It is Joint's second WTA singles title, having already prevailed in Rabat on clay last month.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

At the 2026 Manila 125, Camila Osorio defeated Alexandra Eala in the semifinals [3].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlexandrova stops Eala; Bejlek, Baptiste break new ground in Abu Dhabi\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/news/4445128/alexandrova-stops-eala-bejlek-into-first-wta-semifinal-in-abu-dhabi\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlexandrova stops Eala;\n[...]\nAlexandrova stops Eala; Bejlek, Baptiste break new ground in Abu Dhabi\n[...]\nEkaterina Alexandrova ended Alexandra Eala's run at the Mubadala Abu Dhabi Open in straight sets in the quarterfinals, while Sara Bejlek delivered a flawless performance to reach her first tour-level semifinal.\n[...]\nNo. 2 seed Ekaterina Alexandrova was well-prepared for the challenge that Alexandra Eala -- and the fervent Filipino diaspora crowd -- would pose in the Mubadala Abu Dhabi Open quarterfinals. It showed, as she notched a 6-3, 6-3 win in 1 hour and 30 minutes to reach her first semifinal of 2026.\n[...]\nAbu Dhabi: Scores| Draws| Order of play\n[...]\n### Eala comes from 4-0 down, saves match point in dramatic Abu Dhabi second-round win\n[...]\n\"I saw the last match,\" Alexandrova said in her on-court interview, referring to Eala's remarkable comeback from 4-0 down in the third set -- saving a match point -- the previous day against Aliaksandra Sasnovich.\n[...]\nthe contest remained\n[...]\nlargely owing to Alexandrova\n[...]\nwall. Alexandrova\n[...]\n-- she struck 33 winners to Eala\n[...]\ns 15 --\n[...]\nher to break\n[...]\nThat proved to be the case in the final game of the first set, in which Alexandrova saved four break points before converting her first set point with a simple one-two punch. At 3-3 in the second set, having had her lead pegged back, Alexandrova again found a returning level that was too hot for Eala to handle.\n[...]\nAlexandrova will face Baptiste, who triumphed in her third three-setter of the week 6-2, 4-6, 6-4 over No. 5 seed Liudmila Samsonova. It was the 24-year-old American's second Top 20 win of the tournament -- she also upset No. 4 seed Emma Navarro in the second round -- and the fifth of her career overall.\n[...]\nEkaterina Alexandrova ended Alexandra Eala's run at the Mubadala Abu Dhabi Open in straight sets in the quarterfinals, while Sara Bejlek delivered a flawless performance to reach her first tour-level semifinal.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eXinyu Wang def. Alexandra Eala, 2026 Auckland Semifinal | TennisDB\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://tennis-db.com/wta/matches/balldontlie_wta:334423/xinyu-wang-vs-alexandra-eala\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eXinyu Wang def. Alexandra Eala, 2026 Auckland Semifinal | TennisDB\n\n# Xinyu Wang#57 def. Magda Linette 6-3 6-2 |\n| Jan 7, 2026 | R16 | def. Petra Marcinko 6-0 6-2 |\n| Jan 6, 2026 | R32 | def. Donna Vekic 4-6 6-4 6-4 |\n\n## Related\n\n- All matches from Auckland 2026\n- All editions of Auckland\n- Other WTA 250 matches from 2026\n- All 2026 matches by Xinyu Wang\n- All 2026 matches by Alexandra Eala\n\nSource: BallDontLie WTA API · Updated May 26, 2026\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eHome heartbreak, ranking breakthrough: Alexandra Eala falls in Manila but hits career-high\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://tennisuptodate.com/wta/home-heartbreak-ranking-breakthrough-alexandra-eala-falls-in-manila-but-hits-career-high\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlexandra Eala was eliminated from her home tournament, the WTA 125 Manila Open, after falling in the semifinals to Colombian Camila Osorio. The Filipina had secured comfortable wins in the first two rounds, but ultimately ran into a wall against the South American, a former world No. 33.\n[...]\nThe 20-year-old returned to the Philippines following a quick disappointment at the Australian Open. After her semifinal appearance at the ASB Classic in Auckland, she did not have many days to adapt to Melbourne and was eliminated in the first round of the Australian Open by American Alycia Parks, squandering a one-set lead in a 6-0, 3-6, 2-6 defeat.\n[...]\nEala quickly made her way to Manila thanks to a wildcard from the organisers and entered the\n[...]\nas the second seed. She swiftly showed her favourite status, defeating world No. 163 Alina Charaeva (6-1, 6-2) and world No. 140 Himeno Satasume (6-4, 6-0).\n[...]\nThose wins gave confidence to the\n[...]\nOsorio, the fifth seed and currently ranked world No. 84, a\n[...]\nThe match remained level at 4-4, at which point Osorio secured one final break and fulfilled her responsibility of serving for the match, sealing the victory 6-4, 6-4.\n[...]\nOsorio’s win allows her to continue offsetting the loss of points she will suffer next week, as she was defending points from the second round of the 2025 Australian Open and the quarter-finals of the Singapore Challenger. By reaching the semifinals in Manila, she has ensured valuable points and currently sits at No. 96 in the live rankings.\n[...]\nOn Eala’s side, she departs having climbed positions up to No. 45, and with no points to defend in the coming weeks, all indications suggest she will continue to rise in the rankings — even without major results. Eala’s next commitments will be at the Abu Dhabi Open, starting on Monday after receiving a wildcard into the main draw, followed by the WTA 1000 Doha Open, where she will look to secure a spot through the qualifiers.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEala vs. Alexandrova | Quarterfinals Mubadala Abu Dhabi Open presented by Abu Dhabi Sports Council 2026 | WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/2088/abu-dhabi/2026/scores/LS007\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eEala vs. Alexandrova | Quarterfinals Mubadala Abu Dhabi Open presented by Abu Dhabi Sports Council 2026 | WTA Official\n\nGo back to the home page\n\n-\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEala vs. Wang | Semifinals ASB Classic 2026 | WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/1049/auckland/2026/scores/LS003\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eEala vs. Wang | Semifinals ASB Classic 2026 | WTA Official\n\nGo back to the home page\n\n-\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

Jingshan Open finals slot\n[...]\n# Alex Eala battles Lulu Sun for Jingshan Open finals slot\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala of the Philippines and Lulu Sun of New Zealand clash in the 2025 Jingshan Open semifinals. –AFP FILE PHOTOS\n[...]\n## LIVE: Alex Eala vs Lulu Sun – 2025 Jingshan Open semifinals\n[...]\nMANILA, Philippines–A familiar foe in Lulu Sun of New Zealand stands in the way of Alex Eala’s bid to reach the final of the 2025 Jingshan Tennis Open.\n[...]\nEala and Sun collide on Saturday in the semifinals of the WTA 125 tournament in China.\n[...]\nThe top-seeded Eala advanced after dispatching hometown bet Lu Jiajing, 6-4, 6-1, while No. 3 seed Sun booked her spot with a 6-2, 6-4 victory over Elena Pridankina.\n[...]\nWhile the 20-year-old Filipino has the advantage in the WTA rankings at No. 58 against Sun’s No. 146, the Kiwi player holds the edge in their head-to-head matchup.\n[...]\nThe last time these two players met, Sun got the better of Eala, 7-6(3), 7-5, in the final round of 2024 Wimbledon qualifiers.\n[...]\nwin over Emma\n[...]\nEala, meanwhile, is having a banner year, reaching a career-best No. 56 in June after historic runs in the Miami Open and Lexus Eastbourne Open.\n[...]\nShe is fresh off a Guadalajara 125 title, a second-round appearance at the US Open, and a quarterfinal finish in the Sao Paulo Marino defeated Eala with a score of 1-6, 6-0, 7-6(4) on June 13, 2025 [3][4].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEala vs. Marino | Quarterfinals Lexus Ilkley Open 2025 | WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/1127/ilkley-125/2025/scores/LS004\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eEala vs. Marino | Quarterfinals Lexus Ilkley Open 2025 | WTA Official\n\nGo back to the home page\n\n-\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlex Eala falls to defending champion in Ilkley Open quarters\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.rappler.com/sports/tennis-results-alex-eala-rebecca-marino-ilkley-open-june-13-2025/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlex Eala finds herself in a dogfight against Canadian champion Rebecca Marino before missing a chance to claim a semifinal spot\n[...]\nAlex Eala went through a roller-coaster of a ride in the quarterfinals but faltered in the end game, falling to defending champion Rebecca Marino of Canada, 1-6, 6-0, 7-6(4), in the WTA 125 Lexus Ilkley Open on Friday, June 13, in the United Kingdom.\n[...]\nAfter scoring a breezy 6-1, 6-2 win a day earlier over Valentina Ryser of Switzerland in the second round, the top-seeded Eala found herself in a dogfight against the eighth-seeded Marino at the Show Court 1 of the Ilkley Lawn Tennis and Squash Club.\n[...]\n. The\n[...]\n-year-\n[...]\n, winning the next two\n[...]\nto get back ahead at\n[...]\n5-4.\n[...]\nFacing a 0-40 deficit with\n[...]\nserving for the match, E\n[...]\nsaved 3 match points before eventually breaking Marino to draw level at 5-5. The next two games saw both\n[...]\ndropping serve, sending the match to a tiebreak.\n[...]\nThere would be no repeat of an incredible rally in the tiebreak. From a 2-2 standoff, Marino pulled away and claimed the next 4 points which all but doomed Eala’s hopes for a semifinal berth.\n[...]\nMarino will now advance to the final four where she will face 19-year-old qualifier Celine Naef of Switzerland.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlex Eala falls short vs Canadian foe in Ilkley Open quarterfinals | GMA News Online\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.gmanetwork.com/news/sports/othersports/949336/alex-eala-falls-short-vs-canadian-foe-in-ilkley-open-quarterfinals/story/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlex Eala falls short vs Canadian foe in Ilkley Open quarterfinals | GMA News Online\n\nWe use cookies to ensure you get the best browsing experience. By continued use, you agree to our privacy policy and accept our use of such cookies. For further information, click FIND OUT MORE.\n\nI AGREE FIND OUT MORE\n\nADVERTISEMENT\n\nFiltered By: Sports\n\nSports\n\nPublished June 13, 2025 8:12pm\n\n+ Add GMA on Google\n\nTennis - Madrid Open - Park Manzanares, Madrid, Spain - April 24, 2025 Philippines' Alex Eala reacts during her round of 64 match against Poland's Iga Swiatek REUTERS/Juan Medina\n\nTop-seeded Alex Eala fell short against defending champion Rebecca Marino of Canada, 1-6, 6-0, 7(7)-6(4), in the Ilkley Open quarterfinals on Friday.\n\nAfter dominant wins in the first two sets from each player, the third set became a neck-and-neck affair as Eala and Marino fought for a semis slot.\n\nIn the tiebreaker, the 20-year old Eala had an early 2-1 lead before the 34-year old Marino scored five straight to reach match point.\n\nThe Filipina player, however, salvaged two match points to inch closer but the veteran Marino, ranked 107th in the world, flexed her composure to seal the victory.\n\nPrior to this, world no. 77 Eala defeated Filipino-Aussie Lizette Cabrera and Swiss player Valentina Ryser in the first two rounds of the tournament.\n\n—JKC, GMA Integrated News\n\nTags: Alex Eala, Ilkley Open\n\nMore Videos\n\nMost Popular\n\nLOADING CONTENT\n\nRETRY LOADING\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

[Alexandra Eala Advances to 2026 Philippine Women's Open Quarterfinals](https://justwomenssports.com/reads/alexandra-eala-eliminated-philippine-womens-open-osorio-2026/)\n2. [Philippine Women's Open 2026 Overview | WTA Official](https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/1153/manila-125/2026)\n\nSynthesis:\nIn the 2026 Philippine Women's Open quarterfinals, Camila Osorio defeated Alex Eala with a score of 6-4, 6-4 [1][2].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlexandra Eala Advances to 2026 Philippine Women's Open Quarterfinals\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://justwomenssports.com/reads/alexandra-eala-eliminated-philippine-womens-open-osorio-2026/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlexandra Eala Advances to 2026 Philippine Women's Open Quarterfinals\n[...]\n# Alexandra Eala Falls to Camila Osorio in Philippine Women’s Open Quarterfinals\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala saw her dream of capturing a first WTA title on home soil end Thursday, as the Filipino tennis star fell to Camila Osorio 6-4, 6-4 in the inaugural Philippine Women's Open quarterfinals.\n[...]\nThe matchup pitted the former US Open junior champions against each other in their first professional meeting. However, Alexandra Eala proved unable to overcome the Colombian, as the world No. 49 struggled to find her rhythm against the No. 84.\n[...]\nThe opening set saw three double-faults from each competitor, as both had difficulty finding consistency in their serves. Osorio later broke through in the seventh game of the first set, then held serve to build a 5-3 advantage. Alexandra Eala could not recover, dropping the opening frame 6-4.\n[...]\nThe second set remained competitive through the first eight games, with the score deadlocked at 4-4 after multiple service breaks by both players. However, Osorio elevated her performance at the crucial moment, breaking Alexandra Eala's serve in the ninth game before closing out the match in the 10th.\n[...]\nStatistical analysis revealed tight margins between the players. Both converted second-serve points at an identical 50% rate. The difference emerged on first serves, where Osorio's 59% conversion rate surpassed Alexandra Eala's 51% efficiency, proving decisive in the straight-sets victory.\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala now shifts her focus to the WTA 500 Abu Dhabi Open, set to kick off January 31st. The tournament presents yet another opportunity for the rising Filipina talent to pursue her breakthrough WTA title.\n[...]\nMeanwhile, fifth-seeded Osorio advances to Friday's semifinal against Solana Sierra as she continues her quest for the Philippine Women's Open championship.\n[...]\nAll matches stream live on WTA Unlocked's 125Live.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003ePhilippine Women's Open 2026 Overview | WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/1153/manila-125/2026\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003ePhilippine Women's Open 2026 Overview | WTA Official\n\nGo back to the home page\n\n-\n\nSeed #1\n\neliminated\n\nGER\n\nSeed #2\n\nWild Card\n\neliminated\n\nPHI\n\nSeed #3\n\neliminated\n\nARG\n\nSeed #4\n\nfinalist\n\nCRO\n\nSeed #5\n\nchamp\n\nCOL\n\nSeed #6\n\neliminated\n\nNZL\n\nSeed #7\n\neliminated\n\nSUI\n\nSeed #8\n\neliminated\n\nLAT\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

At the 2025 Miami Open semifinals, Jessica Pegula defeated Alex Eala 7-6(3), 5-7, 6-3 [2][3].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eOsorio ousts Eala in battle  of US Open junior champs - Manila Standard\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://manilastandard.net/sports/314697588/osorio-ousts-eala-in-battle-of-us-open-junior-champs.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eOsorio ousts Eala in battle of US Open junior champs - Manila Standard\n[...]\n# Osorio ousts Eala in battle of US Open junior champs\n[...]\nIn a high-stakes clash between two former US Open junior champions, 2019 winner Camila Osorio emerged victorious Wednesday evening, defeating local favorite Alex Eala at the Rizal Memorial Tennis Center.\n[...]\nThe fifth-seeded Osorio, better known for her prowess on clay, displayed steely resolve to dispatch the 20-year-old Eala—the 2022 US Open junior titlist—with a 6-4, 6-4 victory in the quarterfinals of the 2026 Philippine Women’s Open (WTA 125).\n[...]\nThe match turned into a psychological tug-of-war that peaked in the second set. With the score deadlocked at 4-all in the ninth game, Eala’s nerves faltered. Trailing 15-40, the World No. 49 and second seed committed a costly double fault, handing Osorio a critical 5-4 break.\n[...]\nOsorio, eyeing her fourth career WTA singles title and her first outside Colombia, remained composed. She held serve in the tenth game, sealing the match after Eala netted a forehand return.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003ePhilippine Women's Open 2026 Scores | Latest Scores \u0026 Live Updates – WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/1153/manila-125/2026/scores\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003ePhilippine Women's Open 2026 Scores | Latest Scores \u0026 Live Updates – WTA Official\n\nGo back to the home page\n\n-\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003ePegula vs. Eala | Semifinals Miami Open presented by Itaú 2025 | WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/902/miami/2025/scores/LS003\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003ePegula vs. Eala | Semifinals Miami Open presented by Itaú 2025 | WTA Official\n\nGo back to the home page\n\n-\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

```bash
probe() { url="$1"; code=$(curl -sL -o /tmp/tc.html -w "%{http_code}" -A "Mozilla/5.0" "$url"); if [ "$code" = "200" ] && ! grep -q "Match not found" /tmp/tc.html 2>/dev/null; then echo "OK $url"; else echo "NO $code $url"; fi; }

Ysaline Bonaventure of Belgium advanced to the quarterfinals of the Veneto Open with a 6-2, 6-2 victory over Nuria Brancaccio on Thursday.\n[...]\nBonaventure, who was beaten by eventual winner Alison Van Uytvanck in the second round last year, took just 45 minutes to secure the win.\n[...]\nBonaventure will face American teenager Robin Montgomery in the last eight.\n[...]\nThe 18-year-\n[...]\nMontgomery, who won the girls' singles and doubles titles at the 2021 U.S. Open, continued her impressive form this week with a 6-0, 6-7 (3), 6-0 victory over Laura Pigossi of Brazil.\n[...]\nMontgomery was one of two Americans through to the quarterfinals. Compatriot Ashlyn Krueger progressed after British player Katie Swan lost the opening set and later retired from their second-round encounter with the match poised at 6-3, 4-6, 5-5.\n[...]\nKrueger will next face seventh-seeded Italian Lucrezia Stefanini, who beat Evgeniya Rodina 6-2, 6-4.\n[...]\nIt's the second year of the Veneto Open, the first WTA tournament played on natural grass in Italy. The courts are made on former soccer fields.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eWickmayer to face top-seeded Maria in Veneto Open quarterfinals | Tennis.com\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennis.com/news/articles/wickmayer-to-face-top-seeded-maria-in-veneto-open-quarterfinals\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003emayer to face top\n[...]\nseeded Maria in Veneto Open quarterfinals\n[...]\n## Wickmayer to face top-seeded Maria in Veneto Open quarterfinals\n[...]\n# Wickmayer to face top-seeded Maria in Veneto Open quarterfinals\n[...]\nYanina Wickmayer will face top-seeded Tatjana Maria in the quarterfinals of the Veneto Open after rallying to beat former Australian Open champion Sofia Kenin in three sets\n[...]\nGAIBA, Italy (AP) — Yanina Wickmayer will face top-seeded Tatjana Maria in the quarterfinals of the Veneto Open after rallying to beat former Australian Open champion Sofia Kenin 3-6, 6-4, 7-5 on Wednesday.\n[...]\nKenin appeared headed for victory when she raced to a 4-2 advantage in the third set but the eighth-seeded Wickmayer managed to save a break point, which would have left her American opponent serving for the match.\n[...]\nWickmayer went on to win five of the final six games to keep the Belgian on course for a fourth title of the season.\n[...]\nIt was easier for Maria, who beat Italian wild card Lisa Pigato 6-3, 6-3 to spoil her opponent's 20th birthday celebrations.\n[...]\nWickmayer, who reached the US. Open semifinals in 2009, has lost both her previous matches against Maria.\n[...]\nSixth-seeded Yuan Yue also eased into the quarterfinals with a 6-1, 6-0 victory over Jang Su-jeong.\n[...]\nIt's the second year of the Veneto Open, the first WTA tournament played on natural grass in Italy. These videos feature the key moments from Eala's run to her maiden WTA singles title, which she secured with a 1-6, 7-5, 6-3 comeback victory on September 7, 2025 [3][4][5].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eHISTORY MADE: Alex Eala Claims First WTA Singles Title\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.skramblemedia.com/post/history-made-alex-eala-claims-first-wta-singles-title\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eHISTORY MADE: Alex Eala Claims First WTA Singles Title\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala has been the poster child of Philippine tennis and remains one of the top prospects in the world. Today, she nets her first WTA singles title as she claims the victory over Panna Udvardy in the finals of the Guadalajara 125 Open, also known as the Abierto Zapopan.\n[...]\nThis win comes off Eala’s historic US Open campaign, where she became the first Filipina ever to win a main-draw match at a Grand Slam. Alex Eala steered her momentum straight into Mexico, entering the Guadalajara 125 Open as the No. 2 seed.\n[...]\n. Eala\n[...]\nThe Guadalajara 125 Open finals saw a match-up between familiar foes. Hungarian Panna Udvardy last faced Alex Eala at the Oeiras 125 Open in Portugal, where Udvardy came out on top. The Hungarian seemed determined to keep her record intact against Eala as she took the first set of the final dominantly. Ever determined, Eala bounced back in the second set with a brilliant display. The third and final set remained the only obstacle between defeat and glory. In the end, Philippine pride came out on top as Alex Eala is declared the Guadalajara 125 Open Champion, ending the finals 1-6, 7-5, 6-3.\n[...]\nCourtesy of @gdlopen on Instagram\n[...]\nAt 20 years old, the sky is the limit for Alexandra Eala. Alex continues to prove that she deserves all the hype she’s been receiving, and this finals win solidifies it. Here’s hoping that this win propels her career to new heights, while inspiring Filipino tennis players and the growth of Philippine tennis as a whole.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEala bucks flat start, edges Udvardy in Guadalajara Open to win breakthrough WTA title | bucks flat start, edges Udvardy in Guadalajara Open to win breakthrough WTA title | Philstar.com\n[...]\n# Eala bucks flat start, edges Udvardy in Guadalajara Open to win breakthrough WTA title\n[...]\nAlex Eala poses with her Guadalajara 125 Open trophy.\n[...]\nInstagram / Alex Eala\n[...]\nFilipina tennis ace Alex Eala pulled off the same move in the final of the 2025 Guadalajara Open.\n[...]\nEala finally bagged a Women’s Tennis Association (WTA) title, ruling the 2025 Guadalajara Open after coming from behind against Hungary’s Panna Udvardy, 1-6, 7-5, 6-3 in the Mexico tourney’s final early Sunday morning (Manila time).\n[...]\nEala bucked a very slow start and fought off a furious rally in the second set to bag the championship in the third set.\n[...]\nThe 2\n[...]\n-year-old, ranked 75th in the world, opened sluggishly against the World No. 134 tennister, going down 0-3 in the first set before finally breaking through.\n[...]\nBut Udvardy breezed through to the victory in the first canto as she blanked Eala the rest of the way.\n[...]\n, though, bounced back in the second set, going up 4-1 and ultimately getting\n[...]\n5-3 advantage.\n[...]\n10th\n[...]\nEala, however, forced the third set after clinching the 11th and 12 games.\n[...]\nWith momentum firmly on her side, the Filipina jumped to a 2-0 lead and held the high ground the rest of the way.\n[...]\nWhile Udvardy was able to inch closer, 3-4, Eala won the crucial eighth game to get the 5-3 lead.\n[...]\nEala seized title after she found a hole in the Hungarian’s court, and the former celebrated by throwing her hands up and waving to the crowd.\n[...]\nBefore Sunday’s final, Eala defeated Arianne Hartono, Varvara Lepchenko, Nicole Fossa Huergo and Kayla Day.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlex Eala is finally a WTA champion after topping Guadalajara Open – a WTA champion after topping Guadalajara Open – Tempo\n[...]\nWhat a beautiful and memorable Sunday, Sept. 7, it was for Alex Eala as the country’s newest sports idol finally became a champion.\n[...]\nAnd so Eala etched her name into the history books once again, capturing her maiden Women’s Tennis Association (WTA) title with a stirring 1-6, 7-5, 6-3 comeback victory over Hungary’s Panna Udvardy at the Guadalajara 125 Open.\n[...]\nThat earned Eala distinction as the first Filipina to win a WTA singles crown.\n[...]\nEala had a series of missed opportunities and disappointing finishes, but her Guadalajara 125 Open title could be the start of good things to come.\n[...]\nIt was also a sweet and fulfilling triumph for Eala, who entered the tournament as the second seed with ranking of No. 75.
