En route to this victory, she defeated Kayla Day in the semifinals in straight sets, 6-2, 6-3 [2][3]. Highlights of these matches can be found on the official WTA YouTube channel, which features coverage of both the semifinal against Day and the championship match against Udvardy [4][5][6].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlex Eala vs Panna Udvardy: 2025 Guadalajara 125 Open Live Updates | GMA News Online\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.gmanetwork.com/news/sports/othersports/958285/alex-eala-vs-panna-udvardy-2025-guadalajara-125-open-live-updates/story/?amp=\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlex Eala vs Panna Udvardy: 2025 Guadalajara 125 Open Live Updates | GMA News Online\n\n- GMA Public Affairs\n- Tracking\n\n- Photo\n- Video\n- Radio\n\nSports\n\nTennis - U.S. Open - Flushing Meadows, New York, United States - August 24, 2025 Philippines' Alexandra Eala in action during her first round match against Denmark's Clara Tauson REUTERS/Jeenah Moon\n\n# Alex Eala vs Panna Udvardy: 2025 Guadalajara 125 Open Live Updates\n\nPublished September 07, 2025 12:15 AM By GMA Integrated News\n\nAlex Eala defeated Hungary’s Panna Udvardy, 1-6, 7-5, 6-3 in the Guadalajara 125 Open finals set early Sunday (Philippine time) in Mexico.\n\nHistory was made by the Filipina tennis ace as she claimed her breakthrough WTA crown. The world no. 75 Eala also became the first Filipina to capture a WTA title.\n\nThis was Eala's second shot at WTA title after her finals trip at the Eastbourne Open last June, where her gallant stand fell short after bowing to Maya Joint in the championship round.\n\nThe 20-year-old overcame a sluggish start, mounting a powerful comeback over the next two sets to make history for Philippine tennis.\n\nOTHER STORIES\n\nWho is Panna Udvardy, Alex Eala’s finals opponent in Guadalajara 125 Open?\n\nAlex Eala turns back Panna Udvardy in Guadalajara 125 finals for maiden WTA title\n\nAlex Eala becomes first Filipino to win WTA title\n\nCheck back here for live updates:\n\n—JMB GMA Integrated News\n\nView on GMA News\n\nRELATED VIDEO\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003e2025 Guadalajara 125 Open Finals Results - Tennis Tour Calendar\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://tennistourcalendar.com/2025-guadalajara-125-open-finals-results/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eGuadalajara, Mexico — Filipino tennis phenom Alex Eala has cemented her place in history after capturing her first WTA singles title at the 2025 Guadalajara 125 Open. The 20-year-old World No. 75 overcame a determined Panna Udvardy of Hungary in a thrilling three-set final, 1-6, 7-5, 6-3.\n[...]\nThe championship match, which took place on September 6, 2025, was a testament to Eala’s remarkable resilience and burgeoning talent. Udvardy, the World No. 134, stormed out of the gates, dominating the opening set with a commanding 6-1 scoreline. Despite the lopsided start, Eala, a product of the prestigious Rafael Nadal Academy, refused to be rattled. She clawed her way back in the second set, finding her rhythm and showcasing her powerful forehand to edge out Udvardy 7-5. With the momentum now on her side, Eala carried her stellar play into the deciding third set, where she methodically broke down her opponent’s game to seal the 6-3 victory and secure the biggest title of her career.\n[...]\nThe victory is a watershed moment for both Eala and the Philippines, as she becomes the first Filipino professional player to win a WTA singles title in the Open Era. The tournament victory also capped a flawless week for Eala, who navigated a challenging draw with a clean winning streak. On her path to the final, she defeated Arianne Hartono, Varvara Lepchenko, Nicole Fossa Huergo, and Kayla Day in successive rounds, proving her status as a force to be reckoned with on the WTA 125 tour.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlexandra Eala vs. Kayla Day 05.09.2025 - done

Eala exits after Errani old-school vendetta in Veneto Open quarters\n[...]\nEala exits\n[...]\n-school vendetta in Veneto Open quarters\n[...]\nFilipina teen Alex Eala misses her bid to clinch a breakthrough WTA semifinal spot, finding out quickly that former world No. 5 Sara Errani had brutal revenge in mind\n[...]\nThe 19-year-old Eala fell to 37-year-old former French Open finalist Sara Errani, 6-0, 7-6(3), in the quarterfinal of the WTA Challenger Veneto Open in Gaiba, Italy on Friday, June 21 (Saturday, June 22, Philippine time).\n[...]\nBut the Filipina tennis star immediately discovered Errani was out for some old-school vendetta when the Italian blanked Eala, 6-0, right in the opening set.\n[...]\nThe second set appeared on course for another abbreviated end when Errani opened at 3-1.\n[...]\nErrani then strung together two straight games to retake the lead, but Eala went on a run of her own to once again grab the lead at 6-5.\n[...]\nThe 5-foot-8 Eala had the golden opportunity to extend the match to a deciding third set when she held set point at 40-30, but the seasoned Errani maintained her poise to send the second set to a tiebreak.\n[...]\nFrom there, the Italian seized control and led all throughout the tiebreak to eventually finish the match in one hour and 45 minutes.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult Facing a 1-2 deficit and in danger of losing her grip on the match, Eala stepped on the gas by breaking\n[...]\nthree straight times to claim the next five games and win the set in just eight games, 6-2.\n[...]\nEala broke serve in the fourth game as the world No. 148 Filipino swept the remaining games to finish the set and the match at 6-1 after an hour and 53 minutes.\n[...]\nThe road to the finals will get tougher for Eala. done

verify() { id="$1"; title=$(curl -sL "https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=$id&format=json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('title','?'))" 2>/dev/null); echo "$id | $title"; }

verify uKHblGOhwlY
verify WHTuCQr8Wns

for q in \
  "Alex Eala Sara Errani Veneto Open quarterfinal 2024" \
  "Alex Eala Georgia Pedone Veneto 2024 round 2" \
  "Eala Errani Veneto Open QF 2024 full highlights"; do

done

clinch a breakthrough WTA semifinal spot, finding out quickly that former world No. 5 Sara Errani had brutal revenge in mind\n[...]\nThe 19-year-old Eala fell to 37-year-old former French Open finalist Sara Errani, 6-0, 7-6(3), in the quarterfinal of the WTA Challenger Veneto Open in Gaiba, Italy on Friday, June 21 (Saturday, June 22, Philippine time).\n[...]\nBut the Filipina tennis star retake the lead, but Eala went on a run of her own to once again grab the lead at 6-5.\n[...]\nThe 5-foot-8 Eala had the golden opportunity to extend the match to a deciding third set when she held set point at 40-30, but the seasoned Errani maintained her poise to send the second set to a tiebreak.\n[...]\nFrom there, the Italian seized control and led all throughout the tiebreak to eventually finish the match in one hour and 45 minutes.\n[...]\nIt was another sorry loss for Eala, who was aiming for her first semifinal appearance in a WTA event.\n[...]\nFor the past days, Eala and Errani showed excellent form, with both winning their first two matches in straight sets.\n[...]\nThe Filipina\n[...]\nprevailed over Carole Monet of France in the first round, 6-3, 7-5, then defeated Italian youngster Giorgia Pedone, 6-3, 6-3 in the\n[...]\nErrani opened her campaign in the tournament with a 6-2, 6-4 triumph over Natalija Stevanovic of Serbia, then followed it up with a round of 16 victory against Katarina Zavatska of Ukraine, 7-6(4), 7-5.\n[...]\nist, may have slipped in the singles ladder – now standing at No. 91 – but she remains a force to be reckoned with as proven by Alex Eala eyes best WTA finish in Veneto Open\n[...]\n# ‘Fully focused’ Alex Eala eyes best WTA finish in Veneto Open\n[...]\nFilipina tennis ace Alex Eala looks to stay sharp after booking a quarterfinal spot in the WTA 125 Veneto Open in Italy\n[...]\nBut Alex Eala hopes to continue going after coming out buzzing all match long, defeating fellow 19-year-old Giorgia Pedone of Italy, 6-3, 6-3, to book a quarterfinal spot in the WTA Challenger Veneto Open in Gaiba, Italy, on Thursday, June 20.\n[...]\n“Fully focused for the next round,” Eala posted on her social media account hours after her second-round triumph.\n[...]\nEala needs to stay sharp, much like she did against Pedone, who leaned on home crowd support to wield the upset axe against world No. 96 Hailey Baptiste of the United States, 6-1, 1-0 (retired), in the opening round of the WTA 125 event.\n[...]\nThe world No. 169 Eala proved steadier against Pedone with the match on the line, going up 5-2 in the second set after weathering a string of five straight service breaks exchanged between the two teen standouts.\n[...]\nEala ended the match in an hour and 23 minutes when she held serve in the ninth game.\n[...]\nKnowing she could not afford to give the home crowd a reason to rally behind their compatriot, Eala stepped up her game, breaking the world No. 307 Pedone three straight times to gain a 5-2 lead.\n[...]\nThe Italian got a reprieve when she won the eighth game, but Eala broke serve a fourth time to go up by a set, 6-3.\n[...]\nEala will next face 37-year-old world No. 91 Errani, who like Pedone, will be banking on the support from the stands of her fellow Italians.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlex Eala reaches quarterfinals of Veneto Open in Added YouTube highlight embeds for the **last two matches** in each singles tournament entry, in chronological order (earlier round first, exit round second).

### New embeds added
| Tournament | Videos |
|---|---|
| **Birmingham 2026** | SF vs Masarova, Final vs Bartunkova |
| **Jingshan 2025** | QF vs Lu Jiajing, SF vs Lulu Sun |
| **Sao Paolo 2025** | R16 vs Riera (was missing), QF vs Tjen |
| **Guadalajara 2025** | SF vs Day, Final vs Udvardy |
| **Ilkley 2025** | R16 vs Ryser, QF vs Marino |
| **Canberra 2025** | QF vs Preston, SF vs Wei |

### Order fixed (were reversed)
Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Manila, Auckland, and Miami now list the earlier round first.

### Eastbourne trimmed
Removed the finalist speech embed (`HJOsSm1rnqg`). Kept only the last two **match** highlights: SF vs Gracheva, Final vs Joint.

### Could not add — Veneto 2024
No verified match highlight videos found for R16 vs Pedone or QF vs Errani. The WTA 125 Veneto Open channel has post-match interviews but no official highlight reels for those matches. Left without embeds rather than guess IDs.

### Note on source quality
Most embeds are official WTA/LTA highlights. A few WTA 125 events use the best available verified alternatives:
- **Sao Paolo R16** — full-game highlights (not WTA official)
- **Ilkley QF vs Marino** — fan/news recap (`pTOSrtmzR84`); no official WTA highlight exists
- **Jingshan SF vs Sun** — tournament broadcast-style coverage (not WTA official short)

If you have links for the Veneto matches or prefer to drop the non-official Ilkley/Sao Paolo embeds, I can adjust.

Eala survived a marathon first-round match to upset No. 14 seed Clara Tauson 6-3, 2-6, 7-6 (11), and become the first player from the Philippines in the Open era to win a Grand Slam main-draw match.\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala became the first player from the Philippines in the Open Era to win a Grand Slam main-draw match with her first-round victory Sunday at the US Open.\n[...]\nTrailing 5-1 in the final set, Eala staged a dramatic comeback to force a third-set tiebreak. She needed five match points before ultimately defeating No. 14 seed Clara Tauson, 6-3, 2-6, 7-6[11], to advance to the second round in New York in 2 hours and 36 minutes.\n[...]\nFrom the opening point, the fans embraced the 20-year-old Filipina who broke into the tennis mainstream earlier this year at the WTA 1000 Miami Open. There, she reached the semifinals after defeating Grand Slam champions Jelena Ostapenko, Madison Keys and Iga Swiatek.\n[...]\nprompting a warning to the spectators. When Eala extended\n[...]\nlead to 5-3, the umpire again asked fans to quiet down during points.\n[...]\nTauson raced to a 5-1 lead in\n[...]\nthird and headed for victory. But Eala -- and the crowd -- roared back. She broke Tauson twice while the Dane was serving for the match, the second break coming after a double fault on break point.\n[...]\nDespite her frustration, Tauson saved a match point moments later to force a third-set tiebreak. She rallied from an 8-4-deficit and saved three more match points, but it wasn’t enough to stop Eala from securing her first Grand Slam main-draw win.\n[...]\nIt wasn't her first historic moment in New York, either. In\n[...]\n2022, Eala became the first Filipina player to\n[...]\nsingles title, capturing the US Open girls’ championship.\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala survived a marathon first-round match to upset No. 14 seed Clara Tauson 6-3, 2-6, 7-6 (11), and become the first player from the Philippines in the Open era to win a Grand Slam main-draw match.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eFrom 5-1 down in the third, Alexandra Eala stuns Clara Tauson for first US Open match win | Tennis.com\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennis.com/news/articles/5-1-down-third-set-alexandra-eala-stuns-clara-tauson-first-us-open-match-win\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eFrom 5-1 down in the third, Alexandra Eala stuns Clara Tauson for\n[...]\nUS Open match win | Tennis.com\n[...]\n# From 5-1 down in the third, Alexandra Eala stuns Clara Tauson for first US Open match win\n[...]\nto win a Grand Slam match in the Open Era.\n[...]\nNEW YORK—Alexandra Eala scored her first Grand Slam main-draw victory in thrilling fashion at the 2025 US Open, roaring back from 1-5 down in the final set to stun No. 14 seed Clara Tauson, 6-3, 2-6, 7-6 (11) in the first round on Sunday.\n[...]\nEala burst onto the scene earlier this year when she beat Iga Swiatek to make the semifinals of the Miami Open. At just 20 years old, she survived a third-set tiebreaker against Tauson to become the first Filipino player to\n[...]\na Grand Slam match in the Open Era, advancing in two hours and 36 minutes on Grandstand court.\n[...]\nBut the 2022 girl’s singles champion came to play as the match got underway on Grandstand. Playing in Queens, home to a large Filipino population, she started off by saving four break points in a long third game to break the in-form Tauson for 3-2, breaking a second time to take the opening set.\n[...]\nA finalist at the Dubai Duty Free Tennis Championships back in February, Tauson clicked back into form at the start of the US Open swing with a quarterfinal finish at the Mubadala Citi DC Open and a semifinal run at the Omnium Banque Nationale. The Dane emerged from an exchange of breaks to start the second set and threatened to run away with the match after leveling the contest and taking a 5-1 lead in the third.\n[...]\nEala thrilled the Filipino crowd reeled off five straight games—breaking serve at 4-5 after a lengthy disagreement over a call that required an assist from the still-nascent video review system—and earned the first match point of the thriller when Tauson served at 5-6.\n[...]\nStanding between Eala and a spot in the third round is the winner of the match between Cristina Buçsa and American qualifier Claire Liu.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eUS Open 2025: Rising tennis star Alexandra Eala falls to Spain’s Cristina Bucsa in second round match at Flushing Meadows\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.olympics.com/en/news/us-open-2025-alexandra-eala-falls-to-cristina-bucsa-second-round\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eUS Open 2025: Rising tennis star Alexandra Eala falls to Spain’s Cristina Bucsa in second round match at Flushing Meadows\n[...]\n# US Open 2025: Alexandra Eala falls to Spain’s Cristina Bucsa in second round match at Flushing Meadows\n[...]\nThe Filipino rising star fought valiantly against the unseeded Spanish player, but ultimately fell short in her bid to advance to the third round at the US Open 2025. Read on to learn more.\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala’s run at the 2025 US Open came to a premature close on Wednesday (27 August), as the talented 20-year-old suffered a straight sets defeat 4-6, 3-6 at the hands of Spanish tennis player Cristina Bucsa.\n[...]\nThe match – lasting one hour and 22 minutes – proved to be an enthralling and enlightening affair, as both players grappled over a single ticket to the third round.\n[...]\nAs the partisan crowd threw their weight behind\n[...]\nin the first set.\n[...]\non the back\n[...]\nBusca, who\n[...]\nand surely gained the upper hand when it mattered most.\n[...]\nAnd while the second set brought a renewed sense of hope for the Filipina rising star, she could only watch as the errors began to stack up and the balls began to fall in Busca’s favour, with the scoreline turning from concerning to insurmountable in a matter of minutes.\n[...]\nDefeated, but not dejected, Eala refused to go down with a fight, challenging for each and every point until the match concluded, with the final shot going in Busca’s favour.\n[...]\nAs disappointing as the loss may be for Eala, she leaves Flushing Meadows with history in tow, after becoming the first player from the Philippines to win a Grand Slam match in the Open era, defeating 14th seed Clara Tauson in the first round on Sunday (25 August).\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003e\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.usopen.org/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-26/trailblazing_alexandra_eala_playing_for_more_than_herself_at_the_us_open.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e--\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

Eala, making her Wimbledon debut, won the opening set, but Krejcikova recovered to win the final two sets on Centre Court to advance to the second round [2][4][5]. The match lasted two hours and ten minutes [3][4].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eDefending champ Krejcikova recovers to edge Eala in three sets at Wimbledon\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/news/4299730/defending-champ-krejcikova-recovers-to-edge-eala-in-three-sets-at-wimbledon\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eDefending champ Krejcikova recovers to edge Eala in three sets at Wimbledon\n[...]\n# Defending champ Krejcikova recovers to edge Eala in three sets at Wimbledon\n[...]\nAfter months away with injury, Barbora Krejcikova needed time to settle into her Wimbledon defense before finding her range against Alexandra Eala.\n[...]\nWIMBLEDON -- Given their form coming in, Barbora Krejcikova and Alexandra Eala couldn’t have been on more different trajectories.\n[...]\nKrejcikova, the defending Wimbledon champion and No. 17 seed, missed the first five months of 2025 with a back injury and had to withdraw from this week's Eastbourne quarterfinals citing a thigh injury. She was a modest 3-4 in 2025 since returning from a debilitating back injury.\n[...]\nEala, still\n[...]\nSlam champions --\n[...]\n. At the age of\n[...]\nBut as Tuesday’s match progressed, muscle memory seemed to take over and Krejcikova regained her groove on the grass, defeating Eala 3-6, 6-2, 6-1.\n[...]\nA clean backhand down the line to finish it left Krejcikova -- fist aloft -- roaring in triumph. She now has 14 match-wins at Wimbledon, more than any other Grand Slam.\n[...]\nWith Krejcikova serving at 2-3 in the first set, Eala broke through on the strength of a backhand winner. Playing only the second Grand Slam main draw of her young career, she made that stand up by taking better care of her serve and hitting fewer unforced errors than Krejcikova.\n[...]\nBut the defending champion came screaming back, taking a 5-0 lead in the second set and eventually forcing a decider.\n[...]\nDown 1-0 and facing her second break point, Eala didn’t do enough with an approach shot and couldn’t handle the subsequent volley. In the final analysis, Krejcikova -- an accomplished doubles player -- was better at the net. She won eight of 13 points, while Eala was only 2-for-9.\n[...]\nAfter months away with injury, Barbora Krejcikova needed time to settle into her Wimbledon defense before finding her range against Alexandra Eala.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eWimbledon 2025: Defending champion Barbora Krejcikova avoids first-round upset - BBC Sport\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.bbc.com/sport/tennis/articles/cr4wwpg1lkyo\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eWimbledon 2025: Defending champion Barbora Krejcikova avoids first-round upset - BBC Sport\n[...]\nWimbledon 2025\n[...]\nDates: 30 June-13 July Venue: All England Club\n[...]\nDefending Wimbledon champion Barbora Krejcikova said she is happy to be back playing at the \"temple of tennis\" as she avoided a first-round upset with victory over promising youngster Alexandra Eala.\n[...]\nThe Czech had been an injury doubt coming into the tournament but showed her experience in a 3-6 6-2 6-1 win when she opened Tuesday's play on her return to Centre Court.\n[...]\nMany anticipated her lack of match fitness - she has only played six matches this year - had laid the foundations for an upset at the All England Club against up-and-coming 20-year-old Filipino Eala.\n[...]\nmore history by becoming\n[...]\nthe final of a\n[...]\nThe pair traded breaks early on as Krejcikova's exquisite lob to take Eala's serve was cancelled out by a double fault to put the match back on serve.\n[...]\nMistakes from the reigning champion and an impressive range of shots from Eala secured a second break, and this time Krejcikova could not convert her break-back opportunity, missing the forehand on break point.\n[...]\nAfter a lengthy game at 5-2 where Krejcikova saved a set point, Eala held her nerve to brilliantly serve out the set.\n[...]\nKrejcikova came back in the second set and, while she still looked uncomfortable on serve - frequently redoing her ball toss - she motored to a 5-0 lead.\n[...]\nLooking somewhat disappointed and desperate to avoid a second-set bagel, Eala rallied to hold her serve, then overturned one of the breaks, but could not prevent Krejcikova levelling the match.\n[...]\nA more comfortable third set followed as Krejcikova tidied up the errors and finally showed her emotion as she put herself on the brink of victory by securing the double break for 5-1, letting out a roar after her passing winner.\n[...]\nAnd a similar outburst of emotion followed as she booked her place in the second round.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlex Eala falters after strong start, loses in Wimbledon debut\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/45630738/alex-eala-wimbledon-barbora-krejcikova\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# Alex Eala falters after strong start, loses in Wimbledon debut\n[...]\nDespite a strong start, Alex Eala fell to defending champion Barbora Krejčíková in her Wimbledon debut.\u003e Neil Hall/EPA/Shutterstock\n[...]\n[Alex Eala](https://www.espn.com/sports/tennis/players/profile?playerId=7759)'s long-awaited Grand Slam main draw win will have to wait.\n[...]\nThe 20-year-old Filipina, ranked No. 56 in the world, showed early promise but ultimately bowed out to 2024 Wimbledon Open champion and world No. 16[Barbora Krejčíková](http://www.espn.com/sports/tennis/players/profile?playerId=2042), 6-3, 2-6, 1-6, in the first round of the 2025 Wimbledon Open. It marked a second straight first-round Grand Slam exit for Eala, who also lost at Roland Garros earlier this year against[Emiliana Arango](https://www.espn.com/sports/tennis/players/profile?playerId=3184)of Columbia.\n[...]\nComing in with momentum from a strong grass-court performance in the Eastbourne Open, Eala looked sharp and fearless early. After conceding an early break in the third game, she stormed back with four straight games - highlighted by two breaks of serve - to take a commanding 5-2 lead. Krejčíková, who struggled with five double faults in the set, managed to hold a gritty eighth game, but Eala stayed composed and served it out to take the opener, stunning the Centre Court crowd.\n[...]\nBut the second set saw a dramatic swing in momentum. Krejčíková rediscovered the lethal form that once made\n[...]\n-serve precision finally clicking. Eala managed to earn a break and momentarily threatened a rally, but the Czech responded with a break of her own to level the match at one set apiece.\n[...]\nIn the third, fatigue and errors began to take a toll on Eala. Krejčíková broke early and dominated the remainder of the set, sealing the victory in a grueling two hours and 10 minutes match. Despite the loss, Eala's first-set effort drew praise, showing she can go toe-to-toe with the sport's elite when at her best.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlexandra Eala tests defending Wimbledon champ Barbora Krejcikova in first round | Tennis.com\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennis.com/news/articles/alexandra-eala-tests-defending-wimbledon-champ-barbora-krejcikova-first-round\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# Alexandra Eala tests defending Wimbledon champ Barbora Krejcikova in first round\n[...]\nCentre Court debut.\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala impressed in her Wimbledon debut but fell short of dethroning defending champion Barbora Krecjikova on Centre Court, the 20-year-old ultimately bowing out, 3-6, 6-2, 6-1.\n[...]\nThe phenom from the Philippines arrived for her first main-draw appearance at the All England Club fresh off reaching her first WTA final, but was unable to convert a one-set lead against the former world No. 2, who kicked off her title defense with a two-hour, 10-minute victory.\n[...]\n\"Well, definitely very happy with this win because the match was really tough,\" said Krejcikova. \"It's a first round, so it's obviously very difficult to enter the tournament. But I'm really happy that I got through and I'm in the second round, that yeah, I can keep going.\"\n[...]\nThe Quezon City native, decked out in Nike's homage to the Philippines' national flower, looked to be a dangerous floater against the No. 17th-seeded Krejcikova, who has played few matches in 2025 after winning her second major title at this tournament last summer. The Czech withdrew from Eastbourne after winning two close matches and reaching the quarterfinals, and appeared undercooked in the early goings against Eala.\n[...]\nEala raced to a 5-2 lead and shook off losing a long eighth game—during which Krejcikova saved a set point on her serve—to clinch the opening set on her third opportunity.\n[...]\nKrejcikova caught fire from there, taking a 5-0 lead of her own and held off a resurgent Eala to level the match at one set apiece.\n[...]\nEala and Krejcikova battled through numerous close games as the final set got underway but it was Krejcikova winning the crucial points, nabbing a 5-1, double-break advantage that allowed her to serve for the match.\n[...]\nUp 40-0 on her serve, Krejcikova nailed a backhand down the line and let out a roar of relief, having edged into the second round.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eWimbledon 2025: Barbora Krejcikova comes from set down against Alexandra Eala to get title defence off to winning start | Flashscore.com\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.flashscore.com/news/tennis-wimbledon-wta-singles-krejcikova-comes-from-set-down-against-eala-to-get-title-defence-off-to-winning-start/IJuupcZH/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eWimbledon 2025: Barbora Krejcikova comes from set down against Alexandra Eala to get title defence off to winning start | Flashscore.com\n[...]\n# Krejcikova comes from set down against Eala to get title defence off to winning start\n[...]\nDefending champion Barbora Krejcikova survived a stiff examination from promising 20-year-old Filipina Alexandra Eala before finding her groove to triumph 3-6 6-2 6-1 on her return to Wimbledon's Centre Court on Tuesday.\n[...]\nCzech Krejcikova, a shock winner as 31st seed last year and seeded 17th this time, came into the tournament short on form after a succession of injuries and initially had no answer to the confident, free-swinging Eala, who is the first woman from the Philippines to play at Wimbledon.\n[...]\nEala turned 20 in May and was making her first Wimbledon appearance but she immediately looked at home in the daunting surroundings of the sport's most iconic court.\n[...]\nKrejcikova forced the first break in the third game, however, Eala hit straight back, swinging her lefty forehand freely and throwing in a beautifully disguised drop shot.\n[...]\nShe then upped the ante, holding confidently and breaking again as her two-fisted backhand came to the fore and, from 2-1 down with a break against, Eala reeled off four games in a row.\n[...]\nThe champion just about held serve after six deuces but Eala was not to be denied and served out to take the first set.\n[...]\nWith Eala also starting to lose her radar and with her lightweight serve being punished, the champion swept to a 5-0 lead and took the set 6-2.\n[...]\nKrejcikova grabbed an early break in the third with pounding returns and though Eala had an immediate breakback opportunity, she could not take it and quickly trailed 3-0.\n[...]\nKrejcikova said she was \"super happy\" to even be back to defend her title after a back injury ruled her out of the Australian Open and a thigh problem curtailed her grasscourt build up.\n[...]\nname engraved from last year.\n[...]\nthat opportunity,\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: # Extract WTA match links from tournament pages
for page in \
  "https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/australian-open/2026/scores" \
  "https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/roland-garros/2026/scores" \
  "https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/roland-garros/2025/scores"; do
  echo "=== $page ==="
  curl -sL "$page" -A "Mozilla/5.0" | grep -oE 'href="/tournaments/[^"]*scores/LS[0-9]+"' | grep -i "" | head -3
  curl -sL "$page" -A "Mozilla/5.0" | grep -i "eala" | grep -oE '/tournaments/[^"]*scores/LS[0-9]+' | head -5
done</summary>

```bash
# Extract WTA match links from tournament pages
for page in \
  "https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/australian-open/2026/scores" \
  "https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/roland-garros/2026/scores" \
  "https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/roland-garros/2025/scores"; do
  echo "=== $page ==="
  curl -sL "$page" -A "Mozilla/5.0" | grep -oE 'href="/tournaments/[^"]*scores/LS[0-9]+"' | grep -i "" | head -3
  curl -sL "$page" -A "Mozilla/5.0" | grep -i "eala" | grep -oE '/tournaments/[^"]*scores/LS[0-9]+' | head -5
done
```

The Dane came to New York one of the most in-form players of the summer, having defeated Wimbledon champion Iga Swiatek en route to a semifinal appearance at the WTA 1000 event in Toronto earlier in August. With this win, Eala recorded her fourth triumph over a Top-20 player on the season and became the first player representing the Philippines to win a main-draw match at the US Open.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eTrailblazing Alexandra Eala playing for more than herself at the US Open | Official Site of the 2025 US Open Tennis Championships - A USTA Event\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.usopen.org/amp/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-26/trailblazing_alexandra_eala_playing_for_more_than_herself_at_the_us_open.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eThe first player from the Philippines to ever win a Grand Slam match had just whipped the crowd into a frenzy, rallying from a double break down and converting her fifth match point to prevail over No. 14 seed Clara Tauson—and notch a milestone that will forever be etched into US Open lore.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003ePlayers to watch: Alexandra Eala | Official Site of the 2025 US Open Tennis Championships - A USTA Event\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.usopen.org/amp/en_US/news/articles/2025-06-30/players_to_watch_alexandra_eala.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAlexandra Eala wowed the world at the 2025 Miami Open, riding her wild card all the way to the semifinals of the WTA 1000. At the time, the Filipina was the world No. 140, but jumped all the way to No. 72 after defeating three seeded players, including Australian Open champion Madison Keys, before falling to Jessica Pegula.\n[...]\nHer momentum has continued throughout the spring, and the 20-year-old played in her first WTA final on Saturday at Eastbourne. While she didn’t win the title, Eala enters Wimbledon at a career-high ranking of No. 56, and will face reigning champion Barbora Krejcikova in her first round match on Tuesday.\n[...]\nIn March, she also became the first Filipina to beat a WTA Top 10 (and Top 5) opponent since the WTA rankings began in 1975, defeating Keys during her run in Miami—and then promptly following that up with a two-set win against No. 2 Iga Swiatek. That victory led to her becoming the first player from the Phillippines to reach a WTA Tour-level semifinal. {

    {
      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [Eala upsets Tauson at US Open; 1st from Philippines to win major match - ESPN](https://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/46065849/alexandra-eala-upsets-clara-tauson-us-open-becoming-first-player-philippines-win-major-match)\n2. [From 5-1 down in the third, Alexandra Eala stuns Clara Tauson for first US Open match win | Tennis.com](https://www.tennis.com/news/articles/5-1-down-third-set-alexandra-eala-stuns-clara-tauson-first-us-open-match-win)\n3. [Eala vs. Tauson | Round of 128 US Open 2025 | WTA Official](https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/us-open/scores/LS63131469)\n4. [US Open 2025 results: Alexandra Eala makes history at US Open with win over Clara Tauson - and is 'in the mood for more' - BBC Sport](https://www.bbc.com/sport/tennis/articles/c04ryqwrw1xo)\n5. [](https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2108.html)\n\nSynthesis:\nAt the 2025 US Open, Alexandra Eala defeated Clara Tauson in the first round with a score of 6-3, 2-6, 7-6 (13-11) [1][2]. The match took place on August 24, 2025 [3][4]. Eala rallied from a 5-1 deficit in the third set, aided by a contested video review decision, to become the first player from the Philippines to win a Grand Slam main-draw match in the Open era [1][2][4].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEala upsets Tauson at US Open; 1st from Philippines to win major match - ESPN\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/46065849/alexandra-eala-upsets-clara-tauson-us-open-becoming-first-player-philippines-win-major-match\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eEala upsets Tauson at US Open; 1st from Philippines to win major match - ESPN\n[...]\nNEW YORK -- Alexandra Eala became the first player representing the Philippines to win a Grand Slam match in the Open era, doing so at the US Open by defeating No. 14 seed Clara Tauson 6-3, 2-6, 7-6 (13-11) on Sunday after coming back from a 5-1 deficit in the third set and getting some help from a key video review decision that flustered Tauson.\n[...]\nEala is a 20-year-old who is ranked 75th and trains at the Rafael Nadal Academy. This was her fourth win against a top-20 opponent in 2025, including a victory against Iga Swiatek in March en route to the Miami Open semifinals.\n[...]\nThe pivotal moment came in the last set, with Tauson clinging to a 5-4 lead and serving for the match for the second time. Eala hit a shot right up near the net, and it wasn't immediately clear during play whether or not her racket had crossed over the tape to make contact with the ball, which isn't allowed.\n[...]\nNouni ruled that Eala hit the ball fairly and awarded her the point, giving her two break chances at 15-40. There was a lengthy delay before action resumed, though, with Tauson complaining and telling Nouni: \"Look at the ball. What is your opinion about this? What is your opinion?\"\n[...]\nEven after Nouni's decision was made clear to all, Tauson took some extra time before hitting her next serve, standing with both hands on her hips at the baseline. Eventually, Tauson double-faulted, making it 5-all and drawing a rowdy reaction from a crowd that was loudly supporting Eala.\n[...]\nThat gave Eala 16 of 21 points in that span, and soon she was one away from victory. It took a bit longer, though: In the end, she needed five match points to close out the win after more than 2½ hours.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eFrom 5-1 down in the third, Alexandra Eala stuns Clara Tauson for first US Open match win | Tennis.com\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennis.com/news/articles/5-1-down-third-set-alexandra-eala-stuns-clara-tauson-first-us-open-match-win\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eFrom 5-1\n[...]\nin the third, Alexandra Eala stuns Clara Tauson for\n[...]\n# From 5-1 down in the third, Alexandra Eala stuns Clara Tauson for first US Open match win\n[...]\nmatch in the Open Era.\n[...]\nNEW YORK—Alexandra Eala scored her first Grand Slam main-draw victory in thrilling fashion at the 2025 US Open, roaring back from 1-5 down in the final set to stun No. 14 seed Clara Tauson, 6-3, 2-6, 7-6 (11) in the first round on Sunday.\n[...]\nEala burst onto the scene earlier this year when she beat Iga Swiatek to make the semifinals of the Miami Open. At just 20 years old, she survived a third-set tiebreaker against Tauson to become the first Filipino player to win a Grand Slam match in the Open Era, advancing in two hours and 36 minutes on Grandstand court.\n[...]\nBut the 2022 girl’s singles champion came to play as the match got underway on Grandstand. Playing in Queens, home to a large Filipino population, she started off by saving four break points in a long third game to break the in-form Tauson for 3-2, breaking a second time to take the opening set.\n[...]\nA finalist at the Dubai Duty Free Tennis Championships back in February, Tauson clicked back into form at the start of the US Open swing with a quarterfinal finish at the Mubadala Citi DC Open and a semifinal run at the Omnium Banque Nationale. The Dane emerged from an exchange of breaks to start the second set and threatened to run away with the match after leveling the contest and taking a 5-1 lead in the third.\n[...]\nEala thrilled the Filipino crowd reeled off five straight games—breaking serve at 4-5 after a lengthy disagreement over a call that required an assist from the still-nascent video review system—and earned the first match point of the thriller when Tauson served at 5-6.\n[...]\nR128 - Women's Singles\n[...]\n6 2 7\n[...]\n3 6 6 11\n[...]\nThough she missed out on four total match points, she outrallied the No. 14 seed on her fifth to edge over the finish line, falling to the ground in delight.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEala vs. Tauson | Round of 128 US Open 2025 | WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/us-open/scores/LS63131469\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eEala vs. Tauson | Round of 128 US Open 2025 | WTA Official\n[...]\nEala vs. Tauson | Round of 128 US Open 2025 | WTA Official\n[...]\n288x\n[...]\n.wtat\n[...]\n/2025/0\n[...]\n/24/a446fa\n[...]\ne-014\n[...]\ne3492/WTA_Web_Quick-Links_Tiles-Scores_288x288.png?\n[...]\n=142\u0026\n[...]\n=142)\n[...]\nhead-to-head)\n \nFavorite\n[...]\n2-1\n[...]\nFavorite\n[...]\nA. and is 'in the mood for more' - BBC Sport\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala's three-set victory took two hours and 36 minutes\n[...]\nMeadows, New York Dates:\n[...]\n4 August-\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala made Grand Slam history in front of a raucous US Open crowd on Sunday as she reached the second round of a major for the first time.\n[...]\nThe Filipina was one of the breakout stars of the early part of the season, beating three Grand Slam champions in a row on her way to the Miami Open semi-finals in March.\n[...]\nBut, roared on by a packed Grandstand crowd at Flushing Meadows, Eala stunned world number 14 Clara Tauson 6-3 2-6 7-6 (13-11).\n[...]\nResidents of Little Manila - an area of Queens home to a large Filipino community - packed out the stands to support Eala as she fought back from 5-1 down in the third set.\n[...]\nEala is the first Filipina to win a Grand Slam singles main draw match in the Open era.\n[...]\nEala won the US Open juniors title in 2022, becoming the first Filipino to win a junior Grand Slam singles trophy.\n[...]\nena Ostapenko\n[...]\nShe stayed firm against Tauson, a big hitter who can be wayward, and the 14th seed let her frustrations boil over at a crucial time in the third set.\n[...]\nAfter Eala set up two break points with Tauson leading 5-4 in the decider, the Dane complained that her opponent had hit the ball before it had crossed the net.\n[...]\nShe was booed by the crowd as she argued with umpire Kader Nouni.\n[...]\nIt was so difficult\n[...]\n\"She [Tauson] is a huge player - definitely not an easy draw for a first round but I'm so happy that I could dig deep.\n[...]\n\"I was thinking I had to push the limit, physically and mentally.\"\n[...]\nEala will face either Spanish world number 95 Cristina Bucsa of Spain or American Claire Liu next.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003e\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2108.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e--\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

The final score was 3-6, 6-2, 6-1 [1][3]. After Eala won the opening set, the defending champion Krejcikova recovered to win the next two sets and advance to the second round [2][4].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlex Eala falters after strong start, loses in Wimbledon debut\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/45630738/alex-eala-wimbledon-barbora-krejcikova\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# Alex Eala falters after strong start, loses in Wimbledon debut\n[...]\nDespite a strong start, Alex Eala fell to defending champion Barbora Krejčíková in her Wimbledon debut.\u003e Neil Hall/EPA/Shutterstock\n[...]\n.com/sports\n[...]\n59)'\n[...]\nmain draw win will have\n[...]\nThe 20-year-old Filipina, ranked No. 56 in the world, showed early promise but ultimately bowed out to 2024 Wimbledon Open champion and world No. 16[Barbora Krejčíková](http://www.espn.com/sports/tennis/players/profile?playerId=2042), 6-3, 2-6, 1-6, in the first round of the 2025 Wimbledon Open. It marked a second straight first-round Grand Slam exit for Eala, who also lost at Roland Garros earlier this year against[Emiliana Arango](https://www.espn.com/sports/tennis/players/profile?playerId=3184)of Columbia.\n[...]\nComing in with momentum from a strong grass-court performance in the Eastbourne Open, Eala looked sharp and fearless early. After conceding an early break in the third game, she stormed back with four straight games - highlighted by two breaks of serve - to take a commanding 5-2 lead. Krejčíková, who struggled with five double faults in the set, managed to hold a gritty eighth game, but Eala stayed composed and served it out to take the opener, stunning the Centre Court crowd.\n[...]\nBut the second set saw a dramatic swing in momentum. Krejčíková rediscovered the lethal form that once made her a top-10 player, racing to a 5-0 lead with her tremendous groundstrokes and first-serve precision finally clicking. Eala managed to earn a break and momentarily threatened a rally, but the Czech responded with a break of her own to level the match at one set apiece.\n[...]\nIn the third, fatigue and errors began to take a toll on Eala. Krejčíková broke early and dominated the remainder of the set, sealing the victory in a grueling two hours and 10 minutes match. Despite the loss, Eala's first-set effort drew praise, showing she can go toe-to-toe with the sport's elite when at her best.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eWimbledon 2025: Barbora Krejčíková comes back to begin title defence with win over Alexandra Eala\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.olympics.com/en/news/wimbledon-2025-barbora-krejcikova-alexandra-eala-round-one-results\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eTennis, Wimbledon 2025: Barbora Krejčíková comes back to begin title defence with win over Alexandra Eala - results\n# Wimbledon 2025: Barbora Krejčíková comes back to begin title defence with win over Alexandra Eala\nDefending women's singles champion Krejčíková survived a potential upset after losing the opening set to advance to the second round of the Championships 2025.\n![Barbora Krejcikova of Czechia.](https://img.olympics.com/images/image/private/t_s_16_9_g_auto/t_s_w960/f_auto/primary/ybn3hvgvxabcez9pl3xn)\nPicture by Hannah Peters/Getty Images\nBy Nischal Schwager-Patel\n01 July 2025 15:03 GMT+01 min read\nFrom underdog in 2024 to reigning champion in 2025,[Barbora Krejčíková](https://www.olympics.com/en/athletes/barbora-krejcikova)emerged to a warm applause on a boiling day at[Wimbledon](https://www.olympics.com/en/sport-events/2025-tennis-wimbledon-london).\nThe Czech Olympic[tennis](https://www.olympics.com/en/sports/tennis/)champion was in for a tough opening round against rising Filipina star Alexandra Eala, but Krejčíková came through to win 3-6, 6-2, 6-1 on Tuesday, 1 July.\nKrejčíková will meet Caroline Dolehide or Arantxa Rus in the second round of the Championships 2025.\n*More to follow.*\n[\nLove Tennis?🎾 Relive the best tennis moments from Paris 2024 🏅Get access to unlimited replays, highlights, and live-stream sports events.\n](https://www.olympics.com/en/sign-in?entry_point_type=instory_campaign\u0026entry_point_tag=tennis\u0026template=tennis\u0026origin=https://www.olympics.com/en/olympic-games/paris-2024/videos)\n[\n30 June - 13 JulyTennis | Wimbledon | London| Great Britain\nOngoing event\nTennis\n](https://www.olympics.com/en/sport-events/2025-tennis-wimbledon-london/)\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003e2025 Wimbledon R128: Alexandra Eala vs Barbora Krejcikova Detailed Stats | Tennis Abstract\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennisabstract.com/charting/20250701-W-Wimbledon-R128-Alexandra_Eala-Barbora_Krejcikova.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e2025 Wimbledon R128: Alexandra Eala vs Barbora Krejcikova Detailed Stats | Tennis Abstract\n\n## 2025 Wimbledon R128: Alexandra Eala vs Barbora Krejcikova\n\nBarbora Krejcikova d. Find out how to chart matches yourself| Main Menu |\n| --- |\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlexandra Eala tests defending Wimbledon champ Barbora Krejcikova in first round | Tennis.com\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennis.com/news/articles/alexandra-eala-tests-defending-wimbledon-champ-barbora-krejcikova-first-round\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# Alexandra Eala tests defending Wimbledon champ Barbora Krejcikova in first round\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala impressed in her Wimbledon debut but fell short of dethroning defending champion Barbora Krecjikova on Centre Court, the 20-year-old ultimately bowing out, 3-6, 6-2, 6-1.\n[...]\nThe phenom from the Philippines arrived for her first main-draw appearance at the All England Club fresh off reaching her first WTA final, but was unable to convert a one-set lead against the former world No. 2, who kicked off her title defense with a two-hour, 10-minute victory.\n[...]\n\"Well, definitely very happy with this win because the match was really tough,\" said Krejcikova. \"It's a first round, so it's obviously very difficult to enter the tournament. But I'm really happy that I got through and I'm in the second round, that yeah, I can keep going.\"\n[...]\ntitle defense.\n[...]\nThe Quezon City native, decked out in Nike's homage to the Philippines' national flower, looked to be a dangerous floater against the No. 17th-seeded Krejcikova, who has played few matches in 2025 after winning her second major title at this tournament last summer. The Czech withdrew from Eastbourne after winning two close matches and reaching the quarterfinals, and appeared undercooked in the early goings against Eala.\n[...]\nEala raced to a 5-2 lead and shook off losing a long eighth game—during which Krejcikova saved a set point on her serve—to clinch the opening set on her third opportunity.\n[...]\nKrejcikova caught fire from there, taking a 5-0 lead of her own and held off a resurgent Eala to level the match at one set apiece.\n[...]\nEala and Krejcikova battled through numerous close games as the final set got underway but it was Krejcikova winning the crucial points, nabbing a 5-1, double-break advantage that allowed her to serve for the match.\n[...]\nUp 40-0 on her serve, Krejcikova nailed a backhand down the line and let out a roar of relief, having edged into the second round.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eKrejcikova vs. Eala | Round of 128 The Championships, Wimbledon 2025 | WTA Official\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/904/wimbledon/2025/scores/LS61511608\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eKrejcikova vs. Eala | Round of 128 The Championships, Wimbledon 2025 | WTA Official\n\nEN\n\nEnglish\n\nEspañol\n\nGo back to the home page\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

{

      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [Cristina Bucsa upsets No. 17 seed Elise Mertens at the 2025 US Open | Official Site of the 2025 US Open Tennis Championships - A USTA Event](https://www.usopen.org/amp/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-29/cristina_bucsa_upsets_no_17_seed_elise_mertens_at_the_2025_us_open.html)\n2. [](https://www.usopen.org/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-29/cristina_bucsa_upsets_no_17_seed_elise_mertens_at_the_2025_us_open.html)\n3. [Aryna Sabalenka vs. Cristina Bucsa, 2025 US Open Round 4 | Official Site of the 2025 US Open Tennis Championships - A USTA Event](https://www.usopen.org/amp/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-31/aryna_sabalenka_vs_cristina_bucsa_2025_us_open_round_4.html)\n4. [Defending champ Aryna Sabalenka roars into 2025 US Open quarterfinals | Official Site of the 2025 US Open Tennis Championships - A USTA Event](https://www.usopen.org/amp/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-31/defending_champ_aryna_sabalenka_roars_into_2025_us_open_quarterfinals.html)\n\nSynthesis:\nAt the 2025 US Open, Cristina Bucsa defeated Alexandra Eala in the second round of the women's singles tournament [1][2]. The match took place on Wednesday, August 27, 2025 [2]. Bucsa won in straight sets to advance to the third round [1], where she subsequently defeated Elise Mertens [1][3] before being eliminated in the fourth round by Aryna Sabalenka [4][5].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eCristina Bucsa upsets No. 17 seed Elise Mertens at the 2025 US Open | Official Site of the 2025 US Open Tennis Championships - A USTA Event\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.usopen.org/amp/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-29/cristina_bucsa_upsets_no_17_seed_elise_mertens_at_the_2025_us_open.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eCristina Bucsa upsets No. 17 seed Elise Mertens at the 2025 US Open | Official Site of the 2025 US Open Tennis Championships - A USTA Event\n[...]\n# Cristina Bucsa upsets No. 17 seed Elise Mertens at the 2025 US Open\n[...]\nWHAT HAPPENED: In an entertaining match that featured every shot in the book over three sets, an unflappable Cristina Bucsa of Spain came back to beat Elise Mertens, the No. 17 seed from Belgium, to advance to Round 4 of the 2025 US Open, 3-6, 7-5, 6-3.\n[...]\nThe lesser-known Bucsa, 27, has followed a methodical, steady rise to a place inside the Top 100 for the past three years, and this year had the best results of her career at Grand Slams, reaching Round 3 at Wimbledon and at the 2025 US Open. She seemed unflappable in ousting fan favorite and rising star Alexandra Eala in straight sets in Round 2 on Wednesday to equal that showing.\n[...]\nWhen Mertens was clicking in the first set and a half, Busca looked overmatched, but she kept working on keeping Mertens deep and then moving in to close out rallies. When Mertens’s serve level began to slip in the second set, Bucsa took advantage of the openings, showing surprising power on her forehand, totaling 17 winners with only 7 unforced errors from that wing.\n[...]\nWHAT IT MEANS: Bucsa has a strong forehand and hit 41 winners to Mertens’s 26. She faces a tougher test ahead, seeing either No. 1 seed Aryna Sabalenka or No. 31 seed Leylah Fernandez in Round 4. Mertens’s power bothered her occasionally but her defense is outstanding.\n[...]\nformer Olympic biathlete\n[...]\nearned a bronze in doubles with\n[...]\nSara Sorrib\n[...]\nTormo\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003e\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.usopen.org/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-29/cristina_bucsa_upsets_no_17_seed_elise_mertens_at_the_2025_us_open.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e--\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAryna Sabalenka vs. Cristina Bucsa, 2025 US Open Round 4 | Official Site of the 2025 US Open Tennis Championships - A USTA Event\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.usopen.org/amp/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-31/aryna_sabalenka_vs_cristina_bucsa_2025_us_open_round_4.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAryna Sabalenka vs. Cristina Bucsa, 2025 US Open Round 4 | Official Site of the 2025 US Open Tennis Championships - A USTA Event\n[...]\n# Aryna Sabalenka vs. Cristina Bucsa, 2025 US Open Round 4\n[...]\nDefending champion Aryna Sabalenka left no doubt that she is looking to repeat as champion here at the 2025 US Open. Afterward, however, Sabalenka held in her final two service games to secure Grand Slam match win No. 98 in her already-illustrious career.\n[...]\nWHAT IT MEANS: There’s no such thing as a shoo-in in sports, but anytime a Grand Slam main draw is released, it’s fairly safe to pencil Sabalenka for at least four match victories in any office-pool bracket. import urllib.request, re, json

def check(url):
    try:
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
        html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
        code = 200
    except Exception as e:
        return f"ERR {url}: {e}"
    eala = 'Eala' in html or 'eala' in html.lower()
    title = re.search(r'<title>([^<]+)</title>', html)
    return f"{'OK' if eala else 'NO-EALA'} {url} | {title.group(1)[:80] if title else 'no title'}"

urls = [
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2108.html',
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2109.html',

  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/2025/index.html',
]
for u in urls:
    print(check(u))

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, re, json

def check(url):
    try:
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
        html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
        code = 200
    except Exception as e:
        return f"ERR {url}: {e}"
    eala = 'Eala' in html or 'eala' in html.lower()
    title = re.search(r'<title>([^<]+)</title>', html)
    return f"{'OK' if eala else 'NO-EALA'} {url} | {title.group(1)[:80] if title else 'no title'}"

urls = [
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2108.html',
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2109.html',
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2110.html',
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2208.html',
  'https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/australian-open/scores/LS64000000',
  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/2025/index.html',
]
for u in urls:
    print(check(u))
PY

Her thrilling turnaround semi-final victory over 2022 champion Elena Rybakina was followed by a battle of the first-time finalists against Jasmine Paolini. Amid unbearable tension, the Czech captured the crown to emulate her beloved mentor, the late 1998 Wimbledon champion Jana Novotna, and in so doing, became the second lowest-ranked winner here since WTA rankings began in 1975.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eFrom the Programme: Barbora Krejcikova, a Czech legacy - The Championships, Wimbledon - Official Site by IBM\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wimbledon.com/amp/en_GB/news/articles/2025-06-30/from_the_programme_barbora_krejcikova_a_czech_legacy.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eIn 2024, Krejcikova was not part of the conversation around contenders. Despite being the No.31 seed it had been a challenging first half of the season, featuring a bout of Covid, a bacterial infection and a back injury. Coming into the Fortnight, her win-loss record for the year was 7-9.\n[...]\nA tough first round battle set the tone for what was to follow in a hard-fought campaign. Krejcikova won a tight third set against Veronika Kudermetova before coming through two tie-break sets in the second round against American qualifier Katie Volynets. The second set retirement of her third round opponent Jessica Bouzas Maneiro provided a bit of respite going into the second week.\n[...]\nVictories against Danielle Collins and Jelena Ostapenko were not as comfortable as straight-sets scorelines might suggest, and she had to battle back from a set down in the semi-finals to see off the 2022 champion Elena Rybakina. A 6-2, 2-6, 6-4 win against Jasmine Paolini in the final completed an Open era record in which Krejcikova’s 175 games were the most contested by any competitor in the ladies’ singles.\n[...]\nWimbledon, which\n[...]\nKrejcikova can now look forward to experiencing the honour enjoyed by Novotna in 1999 when she opened play on Day 2 on Centre Court as the reigning champion. A title defence will be challenging after suffering a back injury at the start of the season, and she is sailing against the wind of recent history given there have been seven consecutive first-time champions in the ladies’ singles. But the odds were also not in her favour here 12 months ago, and look how that ended.\n[...]\nThis feature is one of many in the 120-page Official Wimbledon Programme 2025, which is available to purchase here.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

She seemed unflappable in ousting fan favorite and rising star Alexandra Eala in straight sets in Round 2 on Wednesday to equal that showing.\n[...]\nIn their last meeting in Doha in February, Mertens won in straight sets but it says something about Bucsa’s defensive skills that she fought off 13 of 18 break points.\n[...]\nWhen Mertens was clicking in the first set and a half, Busca looked overmatched, but she kept working on keeping Mertens deep and then moving in to close out rallies. She faces a tougher test ahead, seeing either No. 1 seed Aryna Sabalenka or No. 31 seed Leylah Fernandez in Round 4. [Jovic vs. Osaka | Round of 32 Roland Garros 2026 | WTA Official](https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/roland-garros/scores/LS71563874)\n\nSynthesis:\nIva Jovic defeated Alexandra Eala 6-4, 6-2 in the first round of the 2026 Roland Garros [1].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eTakeaways: Jovic beats Eala to set up rematch with Strasbourg champion Navarro\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/news/4509726/takeaways-jovic-beats-eala-to-set-up-rematch-with-strasbourg-champion-navarro\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eIva Jovic defeated Alexandra Eala 6-4, 6-2 in a highly anticipated first-round matchup at Roland Garros on Tuesday. The 17th-seeded American will next face compatriot Emma Navarro in a rematch of their recent meeting in Strasbourg.\n[...]\nNo. 17 seed Iva Jovic came through a highly anticipated first-round showdown against Alexandra Eala in straight sets at Roland Garros on Tuesday. The American will next face compatriot Emma Navarro for the second time in as many weeks.\n[...]\nPlaying on Court 14, Jovic started strongly against Eala. She earned her first break in the fourth game with a forehand winner down the line to move ahead 3-1. Jovic broke again to build a 5-1 lead before briefly losing momentum.\n[...]\nThree double faults from Jovic in the seventh game helped Eala break back. The Filipina broke once more to cut the deficit to 5-4, but Jovic responded immediately with another break to seal the opening set 6-4.\n[...]\nIn the second set, both players exchanged early breaks before Jovic secured another in the sixth game. import urllib.request, re

def fetch(url):
    try:
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
        html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=20).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
        return html
    except Exception as e:
        return f'ERR:{e}'

for url in [
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2108.html',
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2204.html',
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-24/alexandra_eala_upsets_no_14_clara_tauson_at_the_2025_us_open.html',
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-27/alexandra_eala_vs_cristina_bucsa_2025_us_open_round_2.html',
]:
    html = fetch(url)
    if html.startswith('ERR'):
        print(html, url)
        continue
    print('===', url, 'len', len(html))
    for pat in ['Eala', 'Bucsa', 'Tauson', 'Krejcikova', 'title']:
        if pat.lower() in html.lower():
            print(' has', pat)
    m = re.search(r'<title>([^<]+)</title>', html)
    if m: print(' title:', m.group(1)[:100])

# try wimbledon patterns
for url in [
  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/stats/61511608.html',
  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/stats/1701.html',
  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/players/330332/alexandra-eala.html',
]:
    html = fetch(url)
    print('===', url)
    print(html[:200] if not html.startswith('ERR') else html)

python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, re

def fetch(url):
    try:
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
        html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=20).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
        return html
    except Exception as e:
        return f'ERR:{e}'

for url in [
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2108.html',
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/2204.html',
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-24/alexandra_eala_upsets_no_14_clara_tauson_at_the_2025_us_open.html',
  'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/news/articles/2025-08-27/alexandra_eala_vs_cristina_bucsa_2025_us_open_round_2.html',
]:
    html = fetch(url)
    if html.startswith('ERR'):
        print(html, url)
        continue
    print('===', url, 'len', len(html))
    for pat in ['Eala', 'Bucsa', 'Tauson', 'Krejcikova', 'title']:
        if pat.lower() in html.lower():
            print(' has', pat)
    m = re.search(r'<title>([^<]+)</title>', html)
    if m: print(' title:', m.group(1)[:100])

# try wimbledon patterns
for url in [
  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/stats/61511608.html',
  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/stats/1701.html',
  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/players/330332/alexandra-eala.html',
]:
    html = fetch(url)
    print('===', url)
    print(html[:200] if not html.startswith('ERR') else html)

But I'm really happy that I got through and I'm in the second round, that yeah, I can keep going.\"\n[...]\nThe Quezon City native, decked out in Nike's homage to the Philippines' national flower, looked to be a dangerous floater against the No. 17th-seeded Krejcikova, who has played few matches in 2025 after winning her second major title at this tournament last summer. The Czech withdrew from Eastbourne after winning two close matches and reaching the quarterfinals, and appeared undercooked in the early goings against Eala.\n[...]\nEala raced to a 5-2 lead and shook off losing a long eighth game—during which Krejcikova saved a set point on her serve—to clinch the opening set on her third opportunity.\n[...]\nKrejcikova caught fire from there, taking a 5-0 lead of her own and held off a resurgent Eala to level the match at one set apiece.\n[...]\nEala and Krejcikova battled through numerous close games as the final set got underway but it was Krejcikova winning the crucial points, nabbing a 5-1, double-break advantage that allowed her to serve for the match.\n[...]\nUp 40-0 on her serve, Krejcikova nailed a backhand down the line and let out a roar of relief, having edged into the second round.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003e2025 Wimbledon R128: Alexandra Eala vs Barbora Krejcikova Detailed Stats | Tennis Abstract\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.tennisabstract.com/charting/20250701-W-Wimbledon-R128-Alexandra_Eala-Barbora_Krejcikova.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e2025 Wimbledon R128: Alexandra Eala vs Barbora Krejcikova Detailed Stats | Tennis Abstract\n\n## 2025 Wimbledon R128: Alexandra Eala vs Barbora Krejcikova\n\nBarbora Krejcikova d. It marked a second straight first-round Grand Slam exit for Eala, who also lost at Roland Garros earlier this year against[Emiliana Arango](https://www.espn.com/sports/tennis/players/profile?playerId=3184)of Columbia.\n[...]\nComing in with momentum from a strong grass-court performance in the Eastbourne Open, Eala looked sharp and fearless early. After conceding an early break in the third game, she stormed back with four straight games - highlighted by two breaks of serve - to take a commanding 5-2 lead. Krejčíková, who struggled with five double faults in the set, managed to hold a gritty eighth game, but Eala stayed composed and served it out to take the opener, stunning the Centre Court crowd.\n[...]\nBut the second set saw a dramatic swing in momentum. Krejčíková rediscovered the lethal form that once made her a top-10 player, racing to a 5-0 lead with her tremendous groundstrokes and first-serve precision finally clicking. Eala managed to earn a break and momentarily threatened a rally, but the Czech responded with a break of her own to level the match at one set apiece.\n[...]\nIn the third, fatigue and errors began to take a toll on Eala. Krejčíková broke early and dominated the remainder of the set, sealing the victory in a grueling two hours and 10 minutes match. Despite the loss, Eala's first-set effort drew praise, showing she can go toe-to-toe with the sport's elite when at her best.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eHIGHLIGHTS: Alex Eala vs Barbora Krejcikova - Wimbledon R1\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://sports.inquirer.net/629852/live-alex-eala-vs-barbora-krejcikova-wimbledon-first-round\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e- Alex Eala opens her 2025 Wimbledon campaign in the first round against defending champion Barbora Krejcikova.\n- Fresh from her runner-up finish in the Eastbourne Open, Eala is set to compete in her second Grand Slam women’s singles main draw.\n[...]\nKrejcikova needed to overcome a first-set meltdown before taking down the gritty Eala, 3-6, 6-2, 6-1 win to advance to the second round on Tuesday.\n[...]\nMANILA, Philippines — Alex Eala couldn’t translate her strong opening set in her Wimbledon main draw debut after the Czech Republic’s Barbora Krejcikova pulled off a come-from-behind 3-6, 6-2, 6-1 win to advance to the second round.\n[...]\nAfter a blazing first set comeback, Eala lost steam as Krejcikova, the defending champion of the tournament, dominated the last two sets.\n[...]\n- Alex Eala falls to defending champion Barbora Krejcikova in three sets, 6-3, 2-6, 1-6, in the Wimbledon.\n- Krejcikova inches a game closer to a first-round win after surviving Eala in Game 7 for a 5-1 lead.\n[...]\n- Krejcikova denies Alex Eala from another comeback as she takes a 4-1 advantage.\n[...]\n- Alex Eala takes the first set, 6-3, against Krejcikova, the defending champion. She’s one set away from her first ever singles Grand Slam win.\n- Krejcikova hangs on after a grueling eight game to pull within 3-5.\n[...]\n- Four straight games for Alex Eala! She leads 5-2. One more game for the set.\n[...]\n- Eala breaks Krejcikova to lead 4-2. 3 straight game wins for the Filipino.\n[...]\n- Alex Eala holds servce and takes the lead again, 3-2! Error filled game for Krejcikova.\n[...]\n- Alex Eala wins the fourth game to tie the set at 2-2.\n[...]\n- Krejcikova with an early break to take the lead for the first time, 2-1, the opening set.\n[...]\n- Barbora Krejcikova pulls even, 1-1, in Set 1.\n[...]\n- Alex Eala draws first blood against Krejcikova, winning the opening game, 1-0.\n[...]\n## SCHEDULE:\n[...]\nEala at the Wimbledon\n[...]\nJuly 1\n[...]\n- 8:30pm – Alex Eala vs Barbora Krejcikova\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eWimbledon 2025: Barbora Krejčíková comes back to begin title defence with win over Alexandra Eala\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.olympics.com/en/news/wimbledon-2025-barbora-krejcikova-alexandra-eala-round-one-results\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eTennis, Wimbledon 2025: Barbora Krejčíková comes back to begin title defence with win over Alexandra Eala - results\n# Wimbledon 2025: Barbora Krejčíková comes back to begin title defence with win over Alexandra Eala\nDefending women's singles champion Krejčíková survived a potential upset after losing the opening set to advance to the second round of the Championships 2025.\n![Barbora Krejcikova of Czechia.](https://img.olympics.com/images/image/private/t_s_16_9_g_auto/t_s_w960/f_auto/primary/ybn3hvgvxabcez9pl3xn)\nPicture by Hannah Peters/Getty Images\nBy Nischal Schwager-Patel\n01 July 2025 15:03 GMT+01 min read\nFrom underdog in 2024 to reigning champion in 2025,[Barbora Krejčíková](https://www.olympics.com/en/athletes/barbora-krejcikova)emerged to a warm applause on a boiling day at[Wimbledon](https://www.olympics.com/en/sport-events/2025-tennis-wimbledon-london).\nThe Czech Olympic[tennis](https://www.olympics.com/en/sports/tennis/)champion was in for a tough opening round against rising Filipina star Alexandra Eala, but Krejčíková came through to win 3-6, 6-2, 6-1 on Tuesday, 1 July.\nKrejčíková will meet Caroline Dolehide or Arantxa Rus in the second round of the Championships 2025.\n*More to follow.*\n[\nLove Tennis?🎾 Relive the best tennis moments from Paris 2024 🏅Get access to unlimited replays, highlights, and live-stream sports events.\n](https://www.olympics.com/en/sign-in?entry_point_type=instory_campaign\u0026entry_point_tag=tennis\u0026template=tennis\u0026origin=https://www.olympics.com/en/olympic-games/paris-2024/videos)\n[\n30 June - <details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, re

def check(url):
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=20).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    eala = 'Eala' in html or 'eala' in html
    krej = 'Krejcikova' in html or 'krejcikova' in html
    bucsa = 'Bucsa' in html or 'bucsa' in html
    tauson = 'Tauson' in html or 'tauson' in html
    print(f"{url}\n  eala={eala} krej={krej} bucsa={bucsa} tauson={tauson} len={len(html)}")
    # extract json ld or meta
    for m in re.finditer(r'"(?:playerName|name|title)"\s*:\s*"([^"]+)"', html):
        if 'eala' in m.group(1).lower() or 'bucsa' in m.group(1).lower() or 'krejcikova' in m.group(1).lower():
            print(' ', m.group(0)[:120])

for url in [

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, re

def check(url):
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=20).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    eala = 'Eala' in html or 'eala' in html
    krej = 'Krejcikova' in html or 'krejcikova' in html
    bucsa = 'Bucsa' in html or 'bucsa' in html
    tauson = 'Tauson' in html or 'tauson' in html
    print(f"{url}\n  eala={eala} krej={krej} bucsa={bucsa} tauson={tauson} len={len(html)}")
    # extract json ld or meta
    for m in re.finditer(r'"(?:playerName|name|title)"\s*:\s*"([^"]+)"', html):
        if 'eala' in m.group(1).lower() or 'bucsa' in m.group(1).lower() or 'krejcikova' in m.group(1).lower():
            print(' ', m.group(0)[:120])

for url in [
  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/stats/61511608.html',
  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/stats/LS61511608.html',
  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/2025/20250701/LS61511608.html',
  'https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/2025/20250701/index.html',
]:
    try:
        check(url)
    except Exception as e:
        print(url, 'ERR', e)

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, concurrent.futures

def check(id):
    url = f'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/{id}.html'
    try:
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
        html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
        if len(html) < 10000:
            return None
        hits = []
        for name in ['Eala', 'Bucsa', 'Tauson']:
            if name in html:
                hits.append(name)
        if hits:
            return (id, hits, len(html))
    except:
        pass
    return None

# scan likely women's singles R1/R2 range
ids = list(range(2000, 2300))
with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=20) as ex:
    results = [r for r in ex.map(check, ids) if r]
for r in sorted(results):
    print(r)
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, concurrent.futures

def check(id):
    url = f'https://www.usopen.org/en_US/scores/stats/{id}.html'
    try:
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
        html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
        if len(html) < 10000:
            return None
        hits = []
        for name in ['Eala', 'Bucsa', 'Tauson']:
            if name in html:
                hits.append(name)
        if hits:
            return (id, hits, len(html))
    except:
        pass
    return None

# scan likely women's singles R1/R2 range
ids = list(range(2000, 2300))
with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=20) as ex:
    results = [r for r in ex.map(check, ids) if r]
for r in sorted(results):
    print(r)
PY
```
</details>
</tool-use>

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: verify() {
  id="$1"
  json=$(curl -sL "https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=$id&format=json" 2>/dev/null)
  title=$(echo "$json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('title','?'))" 2>/dev/null)
  author=$(echo "$json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('author_name','?'))" 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$id | $author | $title"
}

verify() {
  id="$1"
  json=$(curl -sL "https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=$id&format=json" 2>/dev/null)
  title=$(echo "$json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('title','?'))" 2>/dev/null)
  author=$(echo "$json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('author_name','?'))" 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$id | $author | $title"
}

# Tournament section IDs
for id in kdUsythJKOg -aQ0q3tTvOw vToI4CcaOo8 Z-NN1Y1lknY 8urcN5nHhX4 Cfn0SHv302U iXfxN9Bysvs AIdv9p64R7Y 6j0r-o9cwrw gQZqBwfpXro Nzbi-RyhwZ0 xSZLq-5UK2k Rnq2DZ4Yvi0 Rn6uX8S_6O0 HeE6LMupWSE 38c73sVa8U0 mOD9LLVIbz4 o2djKw4gKF4 J4FwJtkRJho pTOSrtmzR84 maG9TzsuVeM efazYG5hSAA 5JkwaG-t3mE RK9TN4yinAY HhdkG1AwDhU GX43p4YXvL0; do
  verify "$id"
done

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:8]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

queries = [
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Osorio Manila 2026',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Sakatsume Manila 2026',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Lu Jingshan 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Sun Jingshan 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Riera Sao Paulo 2025',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Day Guadalajara',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Udvardy Guadalajara',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Ryser Ilkley',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Marino Ilkley',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Preston Canberra',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Wei Canberra',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Ostapenko Miami 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Vondrousova Montreal 2025',
]

for q in queries:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        author, title = info(vid)
        if any(x in author for x in ['WTA', 'LTA', 'Australian Open', 'Wimbledon', 'US Open', 'Roland', 'Canberra', 'Lexus', 'Eastbourne', 'Tennis Canada']):
            print(f'  OK {vid} | {author} | {title[:65]}')
    print()
PY</summary>
