```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:8]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

queries = [
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Osorio Manila 2026',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Sakatsume Manila 2026',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Lu Jingshan 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Sun Jingshan 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Riera Sao Paulo 2025',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Day Guadalajara',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Udvardy Guadalajara',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Ryser Ilkley',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Marino Ilkley',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Preston Canberra',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Wei Canberra',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Ostapenko Miami 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Vondrousova Montreal 2025',
]

for q in queries:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        author, title = info(vid)
        if any(x in author for x in ['WTA', 'LTA', 'Australian Open', 'Wimbledon', 'US Open', 'Roland', 'Canberra', 'Lexus', 'Eastbourne', 'Tennis Canada']):
            print(f'  OK {vid} | {author} | {title[:65]}')
    print()
```

```
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:8]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&amp;format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

queries = [
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Osorio Manila 2026',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Sakatsume Manila 2026',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Lu Jingshan 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Sun Jingshan 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Riera Sao Paulo 2025',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Day Guadalajara',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Udvardy Guadalajara',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Ryser Ilkley',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Marino Ilkley',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Preston Canberra',
  'WTA 125 Match Highlights Eala Wei Canberra',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Ostapenko Miami 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Vondrousova Montreal 2025',
]

for q in queries:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        author, title = info(vid)
        if any(x in author for x in ['WTA', 'LTA', 'Australian Open', 'Wimbledon', 'US Open', 'Roland', 'Canberra', 'Lexus', 'Eastbourne', 'Tennis Canada']):
            print(f'  OK {vid} | {author} | {title[:65]}')
    print()
PY
```

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:6]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

for q in [
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Ostapenko Miami 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Vondrousova Eala Montreal 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Ostapenko Eastbourne 2025',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        author, title = info(vid)
        print(f'  {vid} | {author} | {title[:70]}')
PY</summary>

```bash
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:6]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

for q in [

]:
```

import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

# targeted searches for Miami Ostapenko WTA
for q in [
  'Jelena Ostapenko vs Alexandra Eala 2025 Miami WTA Match Highlights',
  'Marketa Vondrousova vs Alexandra Eala 2025 Montreal WTA Match Highlights',
  'Alexandra Eala vs Jelena Ostapenko 2025 Miami Open quarterfinal WTA',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        author, title = info(vid)
        if author == 'WTA' or 'Tennis Canada' in author or 'Wimbledon' in author:
            print(f'  OK {vid} | {author} | {title[:75]}')

```bash
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:12]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')
```

queries = [
  'site:youtube.com/@WTA Ostapenko Eala Miami 2025',
  'site:youtube.com/@WTA Eala Guadalajara Udvardy',
  'site:youtube.com/@WTA Eala Guadalajara Day',

]

for q in queries:

    print()

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:15]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

for q in [
  'Ostapenko vs Alexandra Eala 2025 Quarterfinal Miami WTA Match Highlights',
  'Alexandra Eala vs Panna Udvardy Guadalajara WTA Match Highlights',
  'Alexandra Eala vs Kayla Day Guadalajara WTA Match Highlights',
  'Alexandra Eala vs Jelena Ostapenko 2025 Fourth Round Miami WTA',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        author, title = info(vid)
        if author == 'WTA':
            print(f'  WTA {vid} | {title}')
    print()
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:15]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

for q in [
  'Ostapenko vs Alexandra Eala 2025 Quarterfinal Miami WTA Match Highlights',
  'Alexandra Eala vs Panna Udvardy Guadalajara WTA Match Highlights',
  'Alexandra Eala vs Kayla Day Guadalajara WTA Match Highlights',
  'Alexandra Eala vs Jelena Ostapenko 2025 Fourth Round Miami WTA',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        author, title = info(vid)
        if author == 'WTA':
            print(f'  WTA {vid} | {title}')
    print()
PY
```

import re, urllib.request, json

with open('/Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog/alex-eala-pro-career-tracker.md') as f:
    ids = re.findall(r'youtube\s+([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)\s+>', f.read())

OFFICIAL_AUTHORS = {'WTA', 'LTA', 'Australian Open', 'Wimbledon', 'US Open Tennis Championships', 'Tennis Canada', 'Roland-Garros'}

for vid in ids:
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    author = d.get('author_name','?')
    title = d.get('title','?')
    ok = author in OFFICIAL_AUTHORS or author.startswith('WTA') or 'Open' in author and 'Tennis' in author
    status = 'OK' if ok else 'BAD'
    print(f'{status}\t{vid}\t{author}\t{title[:60]}')
print('Total:', len(ids))
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re, urllib.request, json

with open('/Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog/alex-eala-pro-career-tracker.md') as f:
    ids = re.findall(r'youtube\s+([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)\s+>', f.read())

OFFICIAL_AUTHORS = {'WTA', 'LTA', 'Australian Open', 'Wimbledon', 'US Open Tennis Championships', 'Tennis Canada', 'Roland-Garros'}

for vid in ids:
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    author = d.get('author_name','?')
    title = d.get('title','?')
    ok = author in OFFICIAL_AUTHORS or author.startswith('WTA') or 'Open' in author and 'Tennis' in author
    status = 'OK' if ok else 'BAD'
    print(f'{status}\t{vid}\t{author}\t{title[:60]}')
print('Total:', len(ids))
PY
```

Audited every YouTube embed by channel. Removed anything that wasn’t from an official source (WTA, LTA, Grand Slam, or tournament organizer).

### Removed (non-official channels)

| Location | Channel | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| **Manila 2026** (both) | One Sports | Broadcaster recap, not WTA |
| **Jingshan 2025** (both) | CueTV, Nice One | Fan/third-party |
| **Sao Paolo R16** | Nice One | Fan channel (kept WTA QF vs Tjen) |
| **Guadalajara 2025** (both) | CueTV, Tennis Stories | Reuploads, not WTA channel |
| **Ilkley 2025** (both) | Guil Signe, LUWALHATI | Fan/news |
| **Canberra 2025** (both) | Tennis Stories | Not WTA/organizer channel |
| **H2H: Swiatek** | ABS-CBN News | Full-match replay (WTA highlight kept) |
| **H2H: Krejcikova** | SPOTV ASIA | Broadcaster (Wimbledon official kept) |
| **H2H: Keys** | ABS-CBN News | Full-match replay (WTA kept) |
| **H2H: Ostapenko** | ABS-CBN News | No WTA official exists for Miami 2025 |
| **H2H: Pegula** | ABS-CBN News | Duplicate (WTA kept) |

### Still embedded (official only)
All **29** remaining videos are from **WTA**, **LTA**, **Australian Open**, **Wimbledon**, **US Open Tennis Championships**, or **Tennis Canada** (National Bank Open Montreal).

### Tournaments with no official highlights available
These entries no longer have embeds because no official highlight video exists on YouTube:
- Manila 2026, Jingshan 2025, Guadalajara 2025, Ilkley 2025, Canberra 2025, Veneto 2024
- Sao Paolo 2025 (R16 only — QF still has WTA)
- Ostapenko Miami 2025 (H2H section)

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:8]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

BROADCAST = ['One Sports','ABS-CBN','SPOTV','ESPN','BBC','Tennis Channel','Tennis TV','beIN','Eurosport','Rappler','Spin.ph','DZMM','Teleradyo','Fox Sports','Sky Sports','Tennis Australia','Nine','Seven Network','ABC News']

for q in [
  'Alex Eala Canberra Preston ABS-CBN highlights',
  'Alex Eala Canberra Wei highlights One Sports',
  'Alex Eala Ilkley Marino DZMM Teleradyo',
  'Alex Eala Guadalajara Rappler highlights',
  'Alex Eala Jingshan One Sports highlights',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        author, title = info(vid)
        if any(b.lower() in author.lower() or b.lower() in title.lower() for b in BROADCAST) or author in ['WTA','LTA','Australian Open','Wimbledon','US Open Tennis Championships','Tennis Canada']:
            print(f'  {vid} | {author} | {title[:70]}')
    print()
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:8]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

BROADCAST = ['One Sports','ABS-CBN','SPOTV','ESPN','BBC','Tennis Channel','Tennis TV','beIN','Eurosport','Rappler','Spin.ph','DZMM','Teleradyo','Fox Sports','Sky Sports','Tennis Australia','Nine','Seven Network','ABC News']

for q in [
  'Alex Eala Canberra Preston ABS-CBN highlights',
  'Alex Eala Canberra Wei highlights One Sports',
  'Alex Eala Ilkley Marino DZMM Teleradyo',
  'Alex Eala Guadalajara Rappler highlights',
  'Alex Eala Jingshan One Sports highlights',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        author, title = info(vid)
        if any(b.lower() in author.lower() or b.lower() in title.lower() for b in BROADCAST) or author in ['WTA','LTA','Australian Open','Wimbledon','US Open Tennis Championships','Tennis Canada']:
            print(f'  {vid} | {author} | {title[:70]}')
    print()
PY
```

import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

ALLOW = ['WTA','LTA','Australian Open','Wimbledon','US Open','Tennis Canada','One Sports','ABS-CBN','SPOTV','ESPN','BBC','Rappler','Spin','DZMM','Teleradyo','Tennis Channel','beIN','Eurosport','Fox Sports','Sky Sports','Nine','Seven','ABC News','Tennis Australia']

def ok(author, title):
    s = (author + ' ' + title).lower()
    return any(a.lower() in s for a in ALLOW)

for q in [
  'Alex Eala Taylah Preston Canberra One Sports',
  'Alex Eala Valentina Ryser Ilkley Spin.ph',

]:

    print()

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

ALLOW = ['WTA','LTA','Australian Open','Wimbledon','US Open','Tennis Canada','One Sports','ABS-CBN','SPOTV','ESPN','BBC','Rappler','Spin','DZMM','Teleradyo','Tennis Channel','beIN','Eurosport','Fox Sports','Sky Sports','Nine','Seven','ABC News','Tennis Australia']

def ok(author, title):
    s = (author + ' ' + title).lower()
    return any(a.lower() in s for a in ALLOW)

for q in [
  'Alex Eala Taylah Preston Canberra One Sports',
  'Alex Eala Valentina Ryser Ilkley Spin.ph',
  'Alex Eala Guadalajara Udvardy Spin.ph highlights',
  'Alex Eala Guadalajara Day Spin.ph',
  'Alex Eala Jingshan Lulu Sun One Sports',
  'Alex Eala Jingshan Lu One Sports',
  'Alex Eala Julia Riera Sao Paulo One Sports',
  'Alex Eala Julia Riera Sao Paulo ABS-CBN',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        author, title = info(vid)
        if ok(author, title):
            print(f'  {vid} | {author} | {title[:75]}')
    print()
PY
```

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8','replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
```

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import re, urllib.request, json

with open('/Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog/alex-eala-pro-career-tracker.md') as f:
    ids = re.findall(r'youtube\s+([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)\s+>', f.read())

ALLOW = [
    'WTA', 'LTA', 'Australian Open', 'Wimbledon', 'US Open Tennis',
    'Tennis Canada', 'One Sports', 'ABS-CBN', 'SPOTV', 'ESPN', 'BBC',
    'Rappler', 'GMA', 'DZMM', 'Teleradyo', 'Tennis Channel', 'beIN',
    'Eurosport', 'Fox Sports', 'Sky Sports', 'Spin.ph', 'Nine', 'Seven',
]

def allowed(author):
    return any(a.lower() in author.lower() for a in ALLOW)

bad = []
for vid in ids:
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    author = d.get('author_name','?')
    title = d.get('title','?')
    ok = allowed(author)
    print(f"{'OK' if ok else 'BAD'}\t{vid}\t{author}\t{title[:55]}")
    if not ok: bad.append(vid)
print('Total', len(ids), 'Bad', bad)
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re, urllib.request, json

with open('/Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog/alex-eala-pro-career-tracker.md') as f:
    ids = re.findall(r'youtube\s+([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)\s+>', f.read())

ALLOW = [
    'WTA', 'LTA', 'Australian Open', 'Wimbledon', 'US Open Tennis',
    'Tennis Canada', 'One Sports', 'ABS-CBN', 'SPOTV', 'ESPN', 'BBC',
    'Rappler', 'GMA', 'DZMM', 'Teleradyo', 'Tennis Channel', 'beIN',
    'Eurosport', 'Fox Sports', 'Sky Sports', 'Spin.ph', 'Nine', 'Seven',
]

def allowed(author):
    return any(a.lower() in author.lower() for a in ALLOW)

bad = []
for vid in ids:
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    author = d.get('author_name','?')
    title = d.get('title','?')
    ok = allowed(author)
    print(f"{'OK' if ok else 'BAD'}\t{vid}\t{author}\t{title[:55]}")
    if not ok: bad.append(vid)
print('Total', len(ids), 'Bad', bad)
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import re, urllib.request, json

with open('/Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog/alex-eala-pro-career-tracker.md') as f:
    ids = list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'youtube\s+([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)\s+>', f.read())))

HIGHLIGHT_KW = ['highlight', 'highlights', 'h/l', 'match', 'full game', 'full match', 'extended', 'wta match', 'semifinal', 'quarterfinal', 'round of', 'final', 'sf ', 'qf ', 'r16', 'r128', ' vs ', ' v ']
NEWS_KW = ['tv patrol', 'play by play', 'pasok', 'kampeon', 'umusad', 'nato', 'bigong', 'falls short', 'enters', 'advances to', 'bows to', 'radyo', 'teleradyo', '24 oras', 'unang balita', 'headline', 'news', 'replay:', 'documentary', 'group chat', 'vlog']

for vid in ids:
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    author = d.get('author_name','?')
    title = d.get('title','?').lower()
    is_news = any(k in title for k in NEWS_KW) or ('teleradyo' in author.lower())
    is_hl = any(k in title for k in HIGHLIGHT_KW) or author in ['WTA','LTA','Wimbledon','Australian Open','US Open Tennis Championships','Tennis Canada']
    # refine
    if 'tv patrol' in title or 'play by play' in title or 'pasok' in title or 'kampeon sa' in title or 'umusad' in title or 'falls short' in title or 'bows to' in title or 'advances to wta' in title:
        kind = 'NEWS'
    elif 'wta match highlights' in title or 'match highlights' in title or 'extended highlights' in title or 'highlights |' in title or ' h/l' in title or 'full game highlights' in title:
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    elif 'full match' in title or 'replay:' in title:
        kind = 'FULL-MATCH'
    elif author == 'WTA' and (' vs ' in title or ' vs. ' in title):
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    elif author in ['LTA','Wimbledon','Australian Open','US Open Tennis Championships']:
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    elif author == 'SPOTV ASIA' and 'h/l' in title:
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    elif author == 'One Sports' and 'full game highlights' in title:
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    elif author == 'Tennis Canada':
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    else:
        kind = 'REVIEW'
    print(f'{kind}\t{vid}\t{author}\t{d.get("title","")[:70]}')
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re, urllib.request, json

with open('/Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog/alex-eala-pro-career-tracker.md') as f:
    ids = list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'youtube\s+([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)\s+>', f.read())))

HIGHLIGHT_KW = ['highlight', 'highlights', 'h/l', 'match', 'full game', 'full match', 'extended', 'wta match', 'semifinal', 'quarterfinal', 'round of', 'final', 'sf ', 'qf ', 'r16', 'r128', ' vs ', ' v ']
NEWS_KW = ['tv patrol', 'play by play', 'pasok', 'kampeon', 'umusad', 'nato', 'bigong', 'falls short', 'enters', 'advances to', 'bows to', 'radyo', 'teleradyo', '24 oras', 'unang balita', 'headline', 'news', 'replay:', 'documentary', 'group chat', 'vlog']

for vid in ids:
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    author = d.get('author_name','?')
    title = d.get('title','?').lower()
    is_news = any(k in title for k in NEWS_KW) or ('teleradyo' in author.lower())
    is_hl = any(k in title for k in HIGHLIGHT_KW) or author in ['WTA','LTA','Wimbledon','Australian Open','US Open Tennis Championships','Tennis Canada']
    # refine
    if 'tv patrol' in title or 'play by play' in title or 'pasok' in title or 'kampeon sa' in title or 'umusad' in title or 'falls short' in title or 'bows to' in title or 'advances to wta' in title:
        kind = 'NEWS'
    elif 'wta match highlights' in title or 'match highlights' in title or 'extended highlights' in title or 'highlights |' in title or ' h/l' in title or 'full game highlights' in title:
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    elif 'full match' in title or 'replay:' in title:
        kind = 'FULL-MATCH'
    elif author == 'WTA' and (' vs ' in title or ' vs. ' in title):
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    elif author in ['LTA','Wimbledon','Australian Open','US Open Tennis Championships']:
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    elif author == 'SPOTV ASIA' and 'h/l' in title:
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    elif author == 'One Sports' and 'full game highlights' in title:
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    elif author == 'Tennis Canada':
        kind = 'HIGHLIGHTS'
    else:
        kind = 'REVIEW'
    print(f'{kind}\t{vid}\t{author}\t{d.get("title","")[:70]}')
PY
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

NEWS = ['tv patrol','play by play','pasok','kampeon sa','umusad','falls short','bows to','advances to','radyo','24 oras','replay:']

for q in [
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Ostapenko Miami 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Guadalajara Udvardy',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Guadalajara Day',
  'One Sports FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS Eala Canberra Preston',
  'One Sports FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS Eala Ilkley',
  'SPOTV Eala Jingshan highlights',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        a,t = info(vid)
        tl = t.lower()
        if any(n in tl for n in NEWS):
            continue
        if 'highlight' in tl or 'h/l' in tl or (a=='WTA' and ' vs' in tl) or 'full game highlight' in tl:
            ok = a in ['WTA','LTA','Wimbledon','Australian Open','US Open Tennis Championships','One Sports','SPOTV ASIA','Tennis Canada'] or 'Match Highlights' in t
            if ok:
                print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:75]}')
    print()
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

NEWS = ['tv patrol','play by play','pasok','kampeon sa','umusad','falls short','bows to','advances to','radyo','24 oras','replay:']

for q in [
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Ostapenko Miami 2025',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Guadalajara Udvardy',
  'WTA Match Highlights Eala Guadalajara Day',
  'One Sports FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS Eala Canberra Preston',
  'One Sports FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS Eala Ilkley',
  'SPOTV Eala Jingshan highlights',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        a,t = info(vid)
        tl = t.lower()
        if any(n in tl for n in NEWS):
            continue
        if 'highlight' in tl or 'h/l' in tl or (a=='WTA' and ' vs' in tl) or 'full game highlight' in tl:
            ok = a in ['WTA','LTA','Wimbledon','Australian Open','US Open Tennis Championships','One Sports','SPOTV ASIA','Tennis Canada'] or 'Match Highlights' in t
            if ok:
                print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:75]}')
    print()
PY
```

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re, urllib.request, json

with open('/Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog/alex-eala-pro-career-tracker.md') as f:
    ids = list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'youtube\s+([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)\s+>', f.read())))

NEWS = ['tv patrol','play by play','pasok','kampeon sa','umusad','falls short','bows to','advances to wta','radyo','24 oras','replay:','documentary','group chat']

for vid in ids:
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    title = d.get('title','').lower()
    author = d.get('author_name','?')
    bad = any(n in title for n in NEWS) or 'full match' in title and 'highlight' not in title
    print(f"{'REMOVE?' if bad else 'OK'}\t{vid}\t{author}\t{d.get('title','')[:65]}")
print('Total', len(ids))
PY
```

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Updated to **match highlights only** — removed news clips, play-by-play segments, and full-match replays.

### Removed (not highlights)
| Type | Examples removed |
|---|---|
| **TV news** | ABS-CBN TV Patrol (`pasok`, `kampeon`, `umusad`), DZMM Teleradyo |
| **Play-by-play news** | One Sports Canberra “advances to semis” clips |
| **Full-match replays** | ABS-CBN `REPLAY: … FULL MATCH` (Swiatek, Keys, Ostapenko, Pegula) |
| **Full match (non-highlight)** | US Open `Full Match` Tauson embed |

### Kept (actual highlights)
- **WTA / LTA / Grand Slam** — `WTA Match Highlights`, `Extended Highlights`, etc.
- **One Sports** — `FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS` (Manila)
- **SPOTV** — Wimbledon H/L
- **Tennis Canada** — National Bank Open match package
- **US Open** — Highlights + Extended Highlights (Tauson)

### Tournaments with no highlight embeds
No official/broadcaster **highlight** video found for:
- Jingshan 2025, Guadalajara 2025, Ilkley 2025, Canberra 2025, Veneto 2024
- Sao Paolo 2025 R16 (QF WTA highlight kept)
- Ostapenko Miami 2025 (H2H — no WTA highlight exists for that match)

**28** highlight embeds remain, all verified.

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:12]

def info(vid):
    try:
        url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
        d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
        return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')
    except:
        return '?','?'

NEWS = ['tv patrol','play by play','pasok na sa','pasok sa','kampeon sa','umusad','falls short to','bows to','advances to wta','radyo','24 oras','unang balita','headline ngayon']

def is_tennis_content(title, author):
    tl = title.lower()
    if any(n in tl for n in NEWS):
        return False
    tennis_kw = ['highlight','highlights','h/l','full match','full game','replay:',' vs ',' vs. ',' v ','match','wta match','extended']
    return any(k in tl for k in tennis_kw) or author in ['WTA','LTA','Wimbledon','Australian Open','US Open Tennis Championships','Tennis Canada','Tennis TV','beIN SPORTS','SPOTV ASIA','One Sports','ESPN','Tennis Channel']

queries = [
  ('Jingshan', 'Alex Eala Lulu Sun Jingshan WTA 125 highlights'),
  ('Jingshan', 'Alex Eala Jiajing Lu Jingshan highlights WTA'),
  ('SaoPaolo', 'Alex Eala Julia Riera Sao Paulo WTA 250 highlights'),
  ('Guadalajara', 'Alex Eala Kayla Day Guadalajara Open highlights'),
  ('Guadalajara', 'Alex Eala Panna Udvardy Guadalajara final highlights'),
  ('Guadalajara', 'GDL Open Guadalajara Eala Udvardy highlights'),
  ('Ilkley', 'Alex Eala Valentina Ryser Ilkley LTA highlights'),
  ('Ilkley', 'Alex Eala Rebecca Marino Ilkley Open highlights'),
  ('Canberra', 'Alex Eala Taylah Preston Canberra 125 highlights'),
  ('Canberra', 'Alex Eala Sijian Wei Canberra 125 highlights'),
  ('Canberra', 'Workday Canberra International Eala highlights'),
  ('Veneto', 'Alex Eala Sara Errani Veneto Open highlights'),
  ('Veneto', 'Alex Eala Georgia Pedone Veneto highlights'),
  ('Veneto', 'WTA 125 Veneto Eala Errani'),
]

seen = set()
for label, q in queries:
    print(f'=== {label}: {q}')
    for vid in search(q):
        if vid in seen: continue
        a,t = info(vid)
        if is_tennis_content(t,a):
            seen.add(vid)
            print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:80]}')
    print()
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:12]

def info(vid):
    try:
        url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
        d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
        return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')
    except:
        return '?','?'

NEWS = ['tv patrol','play by play','pasok na sa','pasok sa','kampeon sa','umusad','falls short to','bows to','advances to wta','radyo','24 oras','unang balita','headline ngayon']

def is_tennis_content(title, author):
    tl = title.lower()
    if any(n in tl for n in NEWS):
        return False
    tennis_kw = ['highlight','highlights','h/l','full match','full game','replay:',' vs ',' vs. ',' v ','match','wta match','extended']
    return any(k in tl for k in tennis_kw) or author in ['WTA','LTA','Wimbledon','Australian Open','US Open Tennis Championships','Tennis Canada','Tennis TV','beIN SPORTS','SPOTV ASIA','One Sports','ESPN','Tennis Channel']

queries = [
  ('Jingshan', 'Alex Eala Lulu Sun Jingshan WTA 125 highlights'),
  ('Jingshan', 'Alex Eala Jiajing Lu Jingshan highlights WTA'),
  ('SaoPaolo', 'Alex Eala Julia Riera Sao Paulo WTA 250 highlights'),
  ('Guadalajara', 'Alex Eala Kayla Day Guadalajara Open highlights'),
  ('Guadalajara', 'Alex Eala Panna Udvardy Guadalajara final highlights'),
  ('Guadalajara', 'GDL Open Guadalajara Eala Udvardy highlights'),
  ('Ilkley', 'Alex Eala Valentina Ryser Ilkley LTA highlights'),
  ('Ilkley', 'Alex Eala Rebecca Marino Ilkley Open highlights'),
  ('Canberra', 'Alex Eala Taylah Preston Canberra 125 highlights'),
  ('Canberra', 'Alex Eala Sijian Wei Canberra 125 highlights'),
  ('Canberra', 'Workday Canberra International Eala highlights'),
  ('Veneto', 'Alex Eala Sara Errani Veneto Open highlights'),
  ('Veneto', 'Alex Eala Georgia Pedone Veneto highlights'),
  ('Veneto', 'WTA 125 Veneto Eala Errani'),
]

seen = set()
for label, q in queries:
    print(f'=== {label}: {q}')
    for vid in search(q):
        if vid in seen: continue
        a,t = info(vid)
        if is_tennis_content(t,a):
            seen.add(vid)
            print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:80]}')
    print()
```

import urllib.parse, re

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:8]

for q in [
  'Tennis TV Eala Guadalajara',
  'LTA Ilkley Open Eala Ryser highlights',
  'REPLAY Alex Eala Canberra Preston ABS-CBN',
  'REPLAY Alex Eala Ilkley Marino',
  'REPLAY Alex Eala Jingshan Sun ABS-CBN',
  'site:youtube.com/@WTA125 Veneto Eala',
]:
    print('\n===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
        try:
            d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
            t = d.get('title','').lower()
            if 'tv patrol' in t or 'play by play' in t: continue
            if 'highlight' in t or 'replay' in t or 'full match' in t or 'full game' in t:
                print(f'  {vid} | {d.get("author_name")} | {d.get("title")[:75]}')
        except: pass

```bash
import urllib.parse, re

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:8]

for q in [

]:
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Official tennis sites such as WTA, LTA, Grand Slams, and Tennis Canada are being identified as potential highlight sources.

Broadcasters offering actual tennis matches are preferred over news segments. Tennis Stories repackages WTA 125 match highlights directly. Fan channels such as CueTV, Nice One, Guil Signe, and Next Tennis Gems also provide highlight footage. The skill will list these preferred channels in order.

Tennis Stories will be documented as a tier 2 fallback channel. It provides WTA 125 match highlights when official channels lack uploads. The skill will accept highlights, extended highlights, and full match replays. It will reject news segments. Only the last two matches per tournament will be included in chronological order. YouTube oembed verification will be used.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

for q in [
  'REPLAY Alex Eala Guadalajara Udvardy ABS-CBN',
  'REPLAY Alex Eala Sao Paulo Riera ABS-CBN',
  'REPLAY Alex Eala Canberra ABS-CBN',
  'REPLAY Alex Eala Ilkley ABS-CBN',
  'One Sports FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS Eala Riera Sao Paulo',
  'Ilkley Open Eala Marino match highlights',
  'Veneto Open Eala Errani match highlights youtube',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        a,t = info(vid)
        tl = t.lower()
        if 'tv patrol' in tl and 'replay' not in tl: continue
        if 'highlight' in tl or 'replay' in tl or 'full game' in tl or 'full match' in tl or 'match highlights' in tl:
            print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:78]}')
    print()
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, json

def search(q):
    url = 'https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=' + urllib.parse.quote(q)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    return list(dict.fromkeys(re.findall(r'watch\?v=([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})', html)))[:10]

def info(vid):
    url = f'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={vid}&format=json'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
    d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read())
    return d.get('author_name','?'), d.get('title','?')

for q in [
  'REPLAY Alex Eala Guadalajara Udvardy ABS-CBN',
  'REPLAY Alex Eala Sao Paulo Riera ABS-CBN',
  'REPLAY Alex Eala Canberra ABS-CBN',
  'REPLAY Alex Eala Ilkley ABS-CBN',
  'One Sports FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS Eala Riera Sao Paulo',
  'Ilkley Open Eala Marino match highlights',
  'Veneto Open Eala Errani match highlights youtube',
]:
    print('===', q)
    for vid in search(q):
        a,t = info(vid)
        tl = t.lower()
        if 'tv patrol' in tl and 'replay' not in tl: continue
        if 'highlight' in tl or 'replay' in tl or 'full game' in tl or 'full match' in tl or 'match highlights' in tl:
            print(f'  {vid} | {a} | {t[:78]}')
    print()
PY
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Identifying verified tennis highlight channels for the tracker. News content is excluded.

CueTV and Tennis Stories are selected for quarterfinal and semifinal highlights. Nice One is noted for round-of-sixteen. Guil Signe and Next Tennis Gems are non-official options. Marino and Veneto still lack suitable channels.

Channel categories are being defined for the skill. WTA, LTA, Grand Slams, Tennis Canada, SPOTV ASIA, and One Sports are grouped by author or tournament. Tennis Stories covers WTA 125 matches when the official channel lacks a video. CueTV and Nice One serve as fallback compilations for WTA 125 and 250 events when no Tier 1 or 2 highlight exists. Titles must contain "Highlights" or "Full Game Highlights" to qualify.

News and interview channels are excluded. Tier 2 Tennis Stories will cover the Canberra and Guadalajara finals. Tier 3 CueTV will cover the Jingshan quarterfinal. Nice One Full Game Highlights may cover the Guadalajara semifinal and Sao Paulo.</details></think>

### New embeds (verified match footage)

| Tournament | Channel | Match |
|---|---|---|
| **Jingshan 2025** | CueTV | QF vs Lu Jiajing |
| | Tennis Stories | SF vs Lulu Sun |
| **Sao Paolo 2025** | Nice One | R16 vs Riera (`Full Game Highlights`) |
| **Guadalajara 2025** | CueTV | SF vs Kayla Day |
| | Tennis Stories | Final vs Udvardy (`WTA 125 Match Highlights`) |
| **Ilkley 2025** | Guil Signe | R16 vs Ryser (`Match Highlights`) |
| **Canberra 2025** | Tennis Stories | QF vs Preston, SF vs Wei |

### Still no highlight found
- **Ilkley QF** vs Marino
- **Veneto 2024** (both last-two rounds)

### Skill updates (`update-alex-eala-tracker`)

**`SKILL.md`**
- YouTube step links to full rules in `reference.md`
- Last-two-matches embed rule for singles tournaments
- Grand Slam dual-link format (`wtatennis.com` first)
- WTA link order fix (`wtatennis.com` before `tennis.com`)
- Doubles career high corrected to No. 88

**`reference.md`** — new **YouTube embed rules** section:
1. **Official** — WTA, LTA, Grand Slams, Tennis Canada  
2. **Broadcasters** — SPOTV, One Sports (`FULL GAME HIGHLIGHTS`), ABS-CBN/US Open replays & highlights  
3. **Mirrors** — Tennis Stories (`WTA 125 Match Highlights`)  
4. **Regional compilations** — CueTV, Nice One (highlights only)  

**Reject:** TV Patrol, DZMM, “Play by Play” news segments, interviews/vlogs  

**Key rule:** video must show actual tennis — not result-announcement news clips.

---

for top 20, do not show matches. just youtube videos

```json
{
```
```json
  "isFinal": true,
  "references": [
    {
      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [Iva Jovic Defeats Close Friend Alex Eala in Roland Garros 1st Round | TC Live](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n0Aq6ikhs2A)\n2. [Iva Jovic vs Alexandra Eala Live French Open WTA Singles Watchalong Commentary \u0026 Scores](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=09ljI4XhSCA)\n3. [BREAKING: Alex Eala Turns 21 at Roland Garros — The Moment the Whole WTA Stopped Breathing!](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9m6IgpaMLaQ)\n4. [ALEX EALA STUNS Iva Jovic in Paris 😱 French Open Crowd Goes WILD!](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WkUkJOuY0l8)\n\nSynthesis:\nAt the 2026 French Open, Alexandra Eala was defeated by her close friend and frequent doubles partner, Iva Jovic, in the first round [1][2]. Jovic, seeded 17th, won the match in straight sets, securing her spot in the second round with a dominant performance [3]. Despite Eala's effort and a 68% first-serve percentage, Jovic's aggressive baseline play and 34 winners proved too much for the Filipina star to overcome [3]. Eala entered the tournament ranked 37th in the world, hoping to improve upon her first-round exit from the previous year [4][3].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eIva Jovic Defeats Close Friend Alex Eala in Roland Garros 1st Round | TC Live\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n0Aq6ikhs2A\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# Iva Jovic Defeats Close Friend Alex Eala in Roland Garros 1st Round | TC Live\n[...]\n\u003e\u003e ABSOLUTELY. HANDS DOWN. I DON'T THINK I CAN FIND ANYTHING ABOUT EVA JOVIC THAT I DON'T LIKE. AND I THINK HER MENTALITY, HER MATURITY IS TRULY REMARKABLE. AND I ALSO JUST LOVE HER GAME IN GENERAL. I THINK THAT THE WAY THAT SHE IS ABLE TO BE AGGRESSIVE, ESPECIALLY FOR HER STATURE, SHE IS NOT THAT TALL OF A PLAYER AROUND FIVE SIX AND SHE CREATES SO MUCH DEPTH AND SHE'S ABLE TO SPREAD THE COURT AS WELL. AND SHE'S ALSO A HUGE STUDENT OF THE GAME. AND I KNOW THAT SHE LOOKS TO GET BETTER AND BETTER EVERY TIME THAT SHE'S ON THE PRACTICE COURT. SHE HAS A GREAT TEAM BEHIND HER. I'M JUST A HUGE FAN, AND I LOVE TO SEE THAT SHE'S HAVING THIS SUCCESS IN ON CLAY AS WELL, A SURFACE THAT SHE WOULDN'T NECESSARILY, I DON'T THINK SAY THAT SHE LOVES. SO IT'S AWESOME TO SEE THAT SHE WAS UP A SET IN A FIVE THREE AGAINST COCO GAUFF THIS WEEK. AND SO SHE SHE'S FINDING SOME VERY GOOD FORM.\n[...]\nAND YOU KNOW THE AUSTRAL\n[...]\nOPEN RUN OBVIOUSLY THE FALL LAST YEAR WAS WHERE SHE STARTED REALLY FINDING AND MAKING THOSE MOVES UP THE RANKINGS GOT A TITLE AS WELL. BUT THEN THE RUN TO THE QUARTERFINAL AT THE AUSTRALIAN OPEN. AND THEN NOW AS YOU MENTIONED, ALI, ON A SURFACE THAT SHE'S MAYBE NOT AS COMFORTABLE, NATURALLY SHE'S LEARNING AND SHE'S UNDERSTANDING HOW TO PLAY, HOW TO PLAY WITH POSITION, HOW TO STEP IN WHEN SHE NEEDS TO. AND ALSO YOU'RE SEEING A BIT OF HER TENNIS IQ, HOW TO WORK POINTS AND HOW TO GET HER OPPONENTS OUT OF THE COURT. SHE DOESN'T HAVE THE BIGGEST WEAPONS, ALTHOUGH SHE'S CONTINUING TO IMPROVE, BUT SHE'S USING HER COMPLETE GAME. Her recent clay swing includes a strong semifinal appearance where she pushed World No. 5 Jessica Pegula to three sets, alongside notable victories over top-30 players like Anna Kalinskaya. Against Eala, Jovic utilized her recent big-match experience to control the tempo. While Jovic has occasionally struggled against top-10 opposition like Cori Gauff and Aryna Sabalenka this season, she has been ruthlessly efficient against players ranked outside the top 20. For the 37th-ranked Eala, the first-round exit compounds a challenging stretch on clay. Despite securing solid wins over Magdalena Frech and Xinyu Wang earlier in May, the Filipina has struggled to string together consecutive victories, suffering early defeats to Elise Mertens and Leylah Fernandez. Jovic’s straightforward progression reinforces her status as a formidable contender on the surface. Soboleva776632\n\nRelated matches\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eTennis: Alexandra Eala suffers shock exit on historic French Open debut\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.olympics.com/en/news/alexandra-eala-suffers-shock-exit-on-historic-french-open-debut\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eTennis: Alexandra Eala suffers shock exit on historic French Open debut\n[...]\n# Alexandra Eala suffers shock exit on historic French Open debut\n[...]\nFilipina tennis star Eala could not deliver the goods in her Grand Slam main draw debut in Paris as she went down in three sets to Colombia's Emiliana Arango.\n[...]\nAlexandra Eala crashed out of the opening round of the 2025 French Open at Roland-Garros on Sunday (25 May).\n[...]\nJust two days after celebrating her 20th birthday, Eala became the first tennis player from the Philippines to appear in the main draw of a Grand Slam in the Open era.\n[...]\nBut the left-hander's stay in Paris was cut short as she went down 0-6, 6-2, 3-6 to Colombian clay-court specialist Emiliana Arango.\n[...]\nHowever, the errors returned with a vengeance at the start of the third set. She sent a routine forehand volley wide to make it 0-30 with Arango, also making her Slam main draw debut, breaking to love.\n[...]\nAnd with the 2022 US Open junior champion serving to stay at the match at 3-5, more unforced errors handed Arango three match points. Eala saved the first, but a down-the-line forehand saw the Colombian close out victory in an hour and 46 minutes.\n[...]\nArango will now take on Qinwen Zheng after the Olympic champion defeated 2021 runner-up Anastasia Pavlyuchenkova in straight sets.\n[...]\n- French Open 2025 preview, full schedule, and how to watch Roland-Garros action live\n- From Joao Fonseca to Alexandra Eala - 10 rising tennis stars to watch out for at Roland-Garros 2025\n[...]\nRelive the magic ❄️⛷️ Sign up free for full replays, highlights, and medal moments - anytime.\n[...]\n18 May - 8 June 2025Tennis | Roland Garros | Paris | FranceTennis\n[...]\nRoland-Garros preview, schedule and how to watch live6 min read\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eRoland-Garros - YouTube\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.youtube.com/user/rolandgarros\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eRoland-Garros - YouTube\n\nRoland-Garros - YouTube\n[](https://www.youtube.com/)[](https://www.youtube.com/)\n[About](https://www.youtube.com/about/)[Press](https://www.youtube.com/about/press/)[Copyright](https://www.youtube.com/about/copyright/)[Contact us](https://www.youtube.com/t/contact_us/)[Creators](https://www.youtube.com/creators/)[Advertise](https://www.youtube.com/ads/)[Developers](https://developers.google.com/youtube)[Terms](https://www.youtube.com/t/terms)[Privacy](https://www.youtube.com/t/privacy)[Policy \u0026 Safety](https://www.youtube.com/about/policies/)[How YouTube works](https://www.youtube.com/howyoutubeworks?utm_campaign=ytgen\u0026utm_source=ythp\u0026utm_medium=LeftNav\u0026utm_content=txt\u0026u=https://www.youtube.com/howyoutubeworks?utm_source=ythp\u0026utm_medium=LeftNav\u0026utm_campaign=ytgen)[Test new features](https://www.youtube.com/new)[NFL Sunday Ticket](https://tv.youtube.com/learn/nflsundayticket)\n\u0026copy; Linette then held her serve in game five to spark a magnificent turnaround alongside Aoyama, forcing things into a tiebreaker at 6-6, which the pair then won 7-3.\n[...]\nThe second set began in similar fashion, Eala and Martins setting the pace with a blistering start, which they maintained. [Eala, Zarazua bow out of French Open doubles after tough second-round battle](https://tiebreakertimes.com.ph/tbt/eala-zarazua-bow-out-of-french-open-doubles-after-tough-second-round-battle/338882)\n2. [Danilovic/Potapova vs. Eala/Zarazua | Round of 32 Roland Garros 2025 | WTA Official](https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/903/roland-garros/2025/scores/LD60707893)\n3. [French Open, Round 2 – Danilovic / Potapova Won: 6-1, 6-3, Watch Highlights, info and Stats | FanCode](https://www.fancode.com/tennis/tour/roland-garros-2025-18612687/matches/danilovic-potapova-vs-eala-zarazua-127211/live-match-info)\n4. [Women's Tennis Association - Official Website](https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/roland-garros/scores/LD60779833)\n\nSynthesis:\nAlexandra Eala and Renata Zarazua played two matches in the 2025 Roland Garros women's doubles tournament. In the first round, they defeated Emily Appleton and Yvonne Cavalle-Reimers with a score of 7-5, 6-4 [1][2]. They were subsequently eliminated in the second round by Olga Danilovic and Anastasia Potapova, who won the match 6-1, 6-3 [3][2][4].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEala, Zarazua bow out of French Open doubles after tough second-round battle\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://tiebreakertimes.com.ph/tbt/eala-zarazua-bow-out-of-french-open-doubles-after-tough-second-round-battle/338882\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eEala, Zarazua bow out of French Open doubles after tough second-round battle\n[...]\nAlex Eala and Renata Zarazua exited the 2025 French Open women’s doubles tournament in the second round following a 1-6, 3-6 loss to Olga Danilovic and Anastasia Potapova on Friday at Stade Roland Garros in Paris.\n[...]\nThe defeat ended Eala’s historic campaign at the French Open, where she became the first Filipino tennis player — male or female — in the Open era to compete in the singles main draw of a Grand Slam.\n[...]\nComing off a first-round win on Tuesday against Emily Appleton and Yvonne Cavalle-Reimers, Eala and Zarazua showed signs of rust as they were broken early and quickly fell behind 0-4 in the second set after dropping the opening frame.\n[...]\nDanilovic and Potapova dominated with nine winners at that point in the second set, while Eala and Zarazua gave away the fourth game on their serve due to two unforced errors.\n[...]\nThe Filipino-Mexican pair managed to break back in the next game after needing three break points and nearly six minutes of play, with Eala finishing the game to cut the deficit and end their scoring drought.\n[...]\nRiding that momentum, Eala held serve in the next game, with the duo closing the gap to 2-4. They landed 83-percent of their first serves in, putting pressure on their opponents and forcing an unforced error from Potapova to end the game.\n[...]\nHowever, the European tandem quickly reasserted control, holding a love game for the fourth time in the match. The match lasted 1 hour and 25 minutes on Court 4 [1].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eHIGHLIGHTS: Alex Eala, Eva Lys at 2025 Wimbledon Championships doubles\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.rappler.com/sports/results-updates-highlights-alex-eala-eva-lys-ingrid-martins-quinn-gleason-wimbledon-doubles-july-3-2025/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eHIGHLIGHTS: Alex Eala, Eva Lys at 2025 Wimbledon Championships doubles\n[...]\nHIGHLIGHTS: Alex Eala, Eva Lys at 2025 Wimbledon Championships doubles\n[...]\nAlex Eala bids Wimbledon goodbye as she and partner Eva Lys of Germany suffer a first-round exit in the women's doubles competition\n[...]\nMANILA, Philippines – After getting booted out from the women’s singles competition, Alex Eala concluded her Wimbledon run as she and partner Eva Lys of Germany suffered a first-round exit in women’s doubles play on Thursday, July 3.\n[...]\nEala and Lys absorbed a 6-4, 6-2 loss to Brazilian Ingrid Martins and American Quinn Gleason at Court 4.\n[...]\n- Gleason and Martins close out the second set at 6-2\n[...]\nand 25\n[...]\n- Eala and Lys stay alive, holding serve in the seventh game. Score stands at 5-2\n[...]\n5-1\n[...]\nsquander six break points\n[...]\n- Another unconverted break point by Eala and Lys, allowing the American-Brazilian pair of Gleason and Martins to bag the first set at 6-4 after 44 minutes.\n[...]\n- The first set is not\n[...]\ndeficit to 5-4.\n[...]\nbreak points to hold\n[...]\nEva Lys on\n[...]\n, 1-0\n[...]\nAlex Eala will have one more chance to extend history at the sacred grass courts of the 2025 Wimbledon Championships as the Filipina tennis sensation debuts in the doubles event alongside German Eva Lys on Thursday, July 3.\n[...]\nThe pair will take on Brazilian Ingrid Martins and American Quinn Gleason in the opening round at Court 4, two days after Eala made history on Centre Court in a promising 3-6, 6-2, 6-1 loss to defending champion Barbora Krejcikova last Tuesday, July 1.\n[...]\nLys is not far off from world No. 56 Eala in singles competition, currently ranked No. 61. The pair, however, turn to underdogs in doubles play, as Eala stands at a career-high world No. 155, while Lys is way further down at No. 1,486 with a low career-high of 768.\n[...]\nGleason, meanwhile, is ranked No. 70 at doubles, while Martins isn’t far behind at No. 80.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eAlex Eala joins Germany's Eva Lys in Wimbledon doubles\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.gmanetwork.com/news/sports/othersports/951377/alex-eala-joins-germany-s-eva-lys-in-wimbledon-doubles/story/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eFiltered By: Sports\nSports\n# Alex Eala joins Germany's Eva Lys in Wimbledon doubles\n**Published**July 3, 2025 11:31am\n![](https://\n[...]\nmanews.tv\n[...]\nwebpics/2\n[...]\n5/07/\n[...]\n_Eala_at_the_2025_Wimbledon_Championships_2025_07_01_22_18_14.jpg)\n[...]\nAlex Eala's journey in this year's Wimbledon is not yet over.\n[...]\nThe Filipina tennis ace partnered with Germany's Eva Lys to compete in the women's doubles tournament.\n[...]\nEala, WTA no. 56, and Lys will open their doubles campaign tonight at 7:10 p.m. against Brazil's Ingrid Martins and American player Quinn Gleason.\n[...]\nLys, currently the world no. 61, is a 23-year old player who made her professional debut in the ITF Circuit in the United States back in 2016, according to the WTA. ```bash
```
```bash
for url in \
  "https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/stats/61641892.html" \
  "https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/scores/stats/LD61641892.html" \
  "https://www.wimbledon.com/en_GB/matches/61641892.html"; do
  code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "$url")
  echo "$code $url"
done
```

Qinwen Zheng, Bianca Andreescu, Angelique Kerber, and Caroline Wozniacki are new candidates. Emma Navarro and Marta Kostyuk are also being considered.

Iva Jovic should be promoted to the H2H section rather than the watch list, since she has already met Eala in the 2026 RG and at Queen’s. <details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: for slug in qinwen-zheng bianca-andreescu angelique-kerber emma-navarro amanda-anisimova petra-kvitova simona-halep donna-vekic janice-tjen; do
  url="https://www.wtatennis.com/players/$(curl -sL "https://www.wtatennis.com/search?q=$(echo $slug | sed 's/-/%20/g')" 2>/dev/null | grep -oE "/players/[0-9]+/$slug" | head -1 | sed 's|/players/||;s|/$slug||')"
  echo "$slug -> skip"
done
# simpler: construct known IDs from search
for pair in "Qinwen Zheng:316772" "Bianca Andreescu:320962" "Angelique Kerber:318704" "Emma Navarro:329682" "Amanda Anisimova:326384" "Petra Kvitova:316003" "Simona Halep:316316" "Donna Vekic:319034" "Janice Tjen:330444"; do
  name="${pair%%:*}"; id="${pair##*:}"

done</summary>

```bash
for slug in qinwen-zheng bianca-andreescu angelique-kerber emma-navarro amanda-anisimova petra-kvitova simona-halep donna-vekic janice-tjen; do
  url="https://www.wtatennis.com/players/$(curl -sL "https://www.wtatennis.com/search?q=$(echo $slug | sed 's/-/%20/g')" 2>/dev/null | grep -oE "/players/[0-9]+/$slug" | head -1 | sed 's|/players/||;s|/$slug||')"
  echo "$slug -> skip"
done
# simpler: construct known IDs from search
for pair in "Qinwen Zheng:316772" "Bianca Andreescu:320962" "Angelique Kerber:318704" "Emma Navarro:329682" "Amanda Anisimova:326384" "Petra Kvitova:316003" "Simona Halep:316316" "Donna Vekic:319034" "Janice Tjen:330444"; do
  name="${pair%%:*}"; id="${pair##*:}"
  curl -s "https://www.wtatennis.com/head-to-head/330332/$id" -o /dev/null -w "$name ($id): %{http_code}\n"
done
```

Here are additions that fit how the tracker uses this section: **players Eala hasn’t met on the WTA tour yet** (or only in juniors, like Andreeva), with a clear “why I’m watching” note.

### Strong adds (Slam winners / top-tier names, no pro H2H yet)

| Player | Why watch | Draft line |
|---|---|---|
| **Qinwen Zheng** | 2024 Olympic gold; 2024 Roland Garros finalist; likely cross paths as both stay top-20 | `- [Qinwen Zheng](https://www.wtatennis.com/head-to-head/330332/316772) - 2024 Olympic gold and Roland Garros finalist` |
| **Bianca Andreescu** | 2019 US Open champion; similar “breakthrough then rebuild” arc | `- [Bianca Andreescu](https://www.wtatennis.com/head-to-head/330332/320962) - 2019 US Open Winner` |
| **Angelique Kerber** | Three-time Slam winner; classic “can Eala beat a legend?” draw | `- [Angelique Kerber](https://www.wtatennis.com/head-to-head/330332/318704) - 2016 Australian Open, 2016 US Open, and 2018 Wimbledon Winner` |
| **Petra Kvitova** | Two-time Wimbledon champion; big lefty vs lefty story on grass | `- [Petra Kvitova](https://www.wtatennis.com/head-to-head/330332/316003) - 2011 and 2014 Wimbledon Winner` |

### Strong adds (current tour, high odds of a meeting soon)

| Player | Why watch | Draft line |
|---|---|---|
| **Emma Navarro** | Top American in Eala’s ranking band; frequent 1000/Slam draw overlap | `- [Emma Navarro](https://www.wtatennis.com/head-to-head/330332/329682) - 2025 Strasbourg Winner; rising American top-20 fixture` |
| **Amanda Anisimova** | 2025 Wimbledon semifinalist; another American Eala keeps drawing near in big events | `- [Amanda Anisimova](https://www.wtatennis.com/head-to-head/330332/326384) - 2025 Wimbledon semifinalist` |

### Optional (softer, but still on-brand)

| Player | Why watch |
|---|---|
| **Simona Halep** | Two-time Slam winner; only worth it if she’s back playing regularly |
| **Cori Gauff** | Already covered under **Coco Gauff** in H2H — skip |

---

### Move to H2H instead of “Watch Out For”

These have **already happened** — they belong in **Matches Against Other Top 20 Players** (or a new subsection), not the watch list:

| Player | Existing meeting |
|---|---|
| **Iva Jovic** | RG 2026 R1; Queen’s 2026; close friend & doubles partner |
| **Janice Tjen** | Sao Paolo 2025 QF; Abu Dhabi 2026 doubles SF |
| **Donna Vekic** | Birmingham 2026 (Eala’s grass title) |

---

### What I’d actually do on the page

**Keep** the current five (Osaka, Sabalenka, Raducanu, Kenin, Andreeva) — they’re still the right “Slam-champion / legacy” bucket.

**Add** (if you want a tighter list): **Zheng**, **Navarro**, **Andreescu**, and **Jovic** → but Jovic goes to H2H, not here.

**Promote out of watch list** once they play: same rule as Andreeva will eventually need when they meet on the WTA tour again.

Want me to apply a specific subset to the markdown (e.g. add Zheng + Navarro + Andreescu, and promote Jovic/Tjen/Vekic into H2H)?

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: # Search regional broadcasters for gap tournaments
searches=(
  "Eala Jingshan 2025 highlights WTA 125 site:youtube.com"
  "Eala Guadalajara 125 2025 highlights site:youtube.com"
  "Eala Ilkley 2025 highlights LTA site:youtube.com"
  "Eala Canberra 2025 highlights site:youtube.com"
  "Eala Arango Roland Garros 2025 highlights site:youtube.com"
  "Eala Veneto 2024 highlights site:youtube.com"
)
for q in "${searches[@]}"; do
  echo "=== $q ==="
  curl -sL "https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('$q'))")" | grep -oE 'watch\?v=[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11}' | head -6 | sort -u
done</summary>

```bash
# Search regional broadcasters for gap tournaments
searches=(
  "Eala Jingshan 2025 highlights WTA 125 site:youtube.com"
  "Eala Guadalajara 125 2025 highlights site:youtube.com"
  "Eala Ilkley 2025 highlights LTA site:youtube.com"
  "Eala Canberra 2025 highlights site:youtube.com"
  "Eala Arango Roland Garros 2025 highlights site:youtube.com"
  "Eala Veneto 2024 highlights site:youtube.com"
)
for q in "${searches[@]}"; do
  echo "=== $q ==="
  curl -sL "https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('$q'))")" | grep -oE 'watch\?v=[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11}' | head -6 | sort -u
done
```

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: # Broader regional broadcaster searches
for q in \
  "Alexandra Eala Jingshan 2025 beIN highlights" \
  "Eala Guadalajara 2025 WTA final highlights" \
  "Eala Ilkley 2025 LTA highlights" \
  "Eala Canberra 2025 Tennis Australia highlights" \

  "Eala Veneto 2024 highlights WTA"; do

  enc=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('$q'))")

```bash
# Broader regional broadcaster searches
for q in \
  "Alexandra Eala Jingshan 2025 beIN highlights" \
  "Eala Guadalajara 2025 WTA final highlights" \
  "Eala Ilkley 2025 LTA highlights" \
  "Eala Canberra 2025 Tennis Australia highlights" \
  "Eala Arango Roland Garros 2025 beIN highlights" \
  "Eala Veneto 2024 highlights WTA"; do
  echo "=== $q ==="
  enc=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('$q'))")
  curl -sL "https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=$enc" | grep -oE 'watch\?v=[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11}' | head -8 | sort -u
done
```

```bash
# WTA channel searches for specific matches
for q in \
  "site:youtube.com/c/WTA Eala Guadalajara 2025" \
  "site:youtube.com/c/WTA Eala Canberra 2025" \
  "site:youtube.com/c/LTA Eala Ilkley 2025" \
  "site:youtube.com/c/WTA Eala Jingshan 2025" \
  "site:youtube.com/c/rolandgarros Eala Arango 2025"; do
  echo "=== $q ==="
  enc=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('$q'))")
  curl -sL "https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=$enc" | grep -oE 'watch\?v=[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11}' | head -6 | sort -u
done
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: verify() {
  for id in "$@"; do
    curl -s "https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=$id&format=json" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'$id | {d.get(\"author_name\")} | {d.get(\"title\")}')"
  done
}

for q in \
  "Alexandra Eala Panna Udvardy WTA Match Highlights Guadalajara" \
  "Alexandra Eala Rebecca Marino Ilkley LTA highlights" \
  "Alexandra Eala Canberra 125 2025 WTA" \
  "Alexandra Eala Jingshan semifinal 2025" \
  "Alexandra Eala Emiliana Arango Roland Garros highlights beIN" \
  "Alexandra Eala Veneto 2024 WTA 125"; do
  echo "=== $q ==="
  enc=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('$q'))")
  ids=$(curl -sL "https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=$enc" | grep -oE 'watch\?v=[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11}' | head -10 | sort -u)
  echo "$ids" | while read line; do id=${line#watch?v=}; verify "$id"; done
  echo
done</summary>

```bash
verify() {
  for id in "$@"; do
    curl -s "https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=$id&format=json" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'$id | {d.get(\"author_name\")} | {d.get(\"title\")}')"
  done
}

for q in \
  "Alexandra Eala Panna Udvardy WTA Match Highlights Guadalajara" \
  "Alexandra Eala Rebecca Marino Ilkley LTA highlights" \
  "Alexandra Eala Canberra 125 2025 WTA" \
  "Alexandra Eala Jingshan semifinal 2025" \
  "Alexandra Eala Emiliana Arango Roland Garros highlights beIN" \
  "Alexandra Eala Veneto 2024 WTA 125"; do
  echo "=== $q ==="
  enc=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('$q'))")
  ids=$(curl -sL "https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=$enc" | grep -oE 'watch\?v=[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11}' | head -10 | sort -u)
  echo "$ids" | while read line; do id=${line#watch?v=}; verify "$id"; done
  echo
done
```

```
=== Alexandra Eala Panna Udvardy WTA Match Highlights Guadalajara ===

```
</details>
</tool-use>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Updated the tracker skill to search **all regional rights-holding broadcasters**, not just the old short list (SPOTV, One Sports, ABS-CBN).

### Policy changes (`reference.md` + `SKILL.md`)

**Accept tier 2** is now any verified **rights-holding broadcaster in any region**, using clear acceptance criteria (known broadcaster channel, title names the match, actual highlight/replay footage).

**New sections:**
- **Broadcaster search strategy** — multiple query patterns per gap match, including regional broadcaster names and official channel scoped searches
- **Broadcaster acceptance criteria** — how to distinguish legitimate broadcast uploads from news/fan clips
- **Expanded quick reference** — examples by region (Americas, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Middle East, Philippines, Canada, Australia)

**Reject list expanded** with more known fan mirrors: `stateofsport211`, `Next Tennis Gems`, etc.

**SKILL.md Step 6** now explicitly says: search official channels first, then broadcasters globally before leaving a gap.

### Tracker impact

Re-searched the still-empty blocks (Jingshan, Guadalajara, Ilkley, Canberra, Veneto, 2025 RG vs Arango) under the broader policy. **No new legitimate broadcaster uploads found on YouTube** — only fan reuploads and news clips, which stay excluded.

The **2026 RG beIN SPORTS Asia** embed (`WRTmI2IH0is`) already fits the updated policy and stays.

Future tracker updates will run the global broadcaster search before omitting an embed.

---

```bash
for q in "Iva Jovic Alexandra Eala Roland Garros 2026 beIN highlights" "Iva Jovic Alexandra Eala London 2026 WTA Match Highlights" "Jovic Eala Queen's 2026 highlights"; do
  echo "=== $q ==="
  enc=$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('$q'))")
  curl -sL "https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=$enc" | grep -oE 'watch\?v=[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11}' | head -6 | sort -u
done
for id in WRTmI2IH0is kv9jOR3P7O0; do
  curl -s "https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=$id&format=json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'$id | {d.get(\"author_name\")} | {d.get(\"title\")}')"
done
```
